Ayah

سُورَةٌ أَنزَلْنَـٰهَا وَفَرَضْنَـٰهَا وَأَنزَلْنَا فِيهَآ ءَايَـٰتٍۭ بَيِّنَـٰتٍ لَّعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ (۱)
[This is] a surah which We have sent down and prescribed, and We have sent down in it manifest signs so that you may take admonition. (1)

Key Points

The word "Sūrah" is a designation from Allah for a collection of verses (āyāt) from the Holy Quran. «سورة... انزلنا فیها آیات»

Messages

1- Rulings (Aḥkām) have been made obligatory by the command of Allah. «فرضناها»

2- The Quran is the mandatory and executive law of the religion. «فرضناها»

3- The verses of the Quran are clear and understandable. «فیها آیات بینات» (Although understanding some parts of the verses requires research and exegesis/Tafsir.)

4- Humanity is in need of admonition and remembrance (Tadhakkur). «لعلّكم تذكّرون»

5- The principles of Quranic knowledge are rooted in human nature (fiṭrah), and through remembrance (Tadhakkur), the veil of negligence is lifted. «تذكّرون»

Explanations

| Portrait of Sūrat An-Nūr This Sūrah has sixty-four verses and was revealed in Medina. The reason for naming this Sūrah "An-Nūr" (The Light) is its thirty-fifth verse, which introduces Allah as the Light of the heavens and the earth. Since this Sūrah contains many recommendations regarding the observance of chastity and purity, traditions (Riwāyāt) have emphasized that women should learn and recite this Sūrah. The rulings concerning the punishment of adulterous men and women, and those who accuse chaste women of adultery, as well as the famous incident of "Al-Ifk" (the slander), the ruling on Hijab, and the observance of chastity to prevent deviations, are other topics addressed by this Sūrah.

Ayah

ٱلزَّانِيَةُ وَٱلزَّانِى فَٱجْلِدُوا۟ كُلَّ وَٰحِدٍ مِّنْهُمَا مِا۟ئَةَ جَلْدَةٍ ۖ وَلَا تَأْخُذْكُم بِهِمَا رَأْفَةٌ فِى دِينِ ٱللَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ تُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ ۖ وَلْيَشْهَدْ عَذَابَهُمَا طَآئِفَةٌ مِّنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۲)
As for the fornicatress and the fornicator, strike each of them a hundred lashes, and let not pity for them overcome you in Allah’s law, if you believe in Allah and the Last Day, and let their punishment be witnessed by a group of the faithful. (2)

Key Points

The rulings regarding zina (fornication/adultery) differ based on the circumstances of the individuals. This verse only refers to one situation: if an unmarried man or woman commits zina, they receive one hundred lashes. However, if someone who has a spouse and access to Him commits zina, they are subject to rajm (stoning).

Mischiefs of Zina in the Quran and Narrations The Quran considers zina an ugly deed and prohibits approaching it. «ولاتقربوا الزّنا»(1) It counts the abandonment of zina as a sign of Allah’s true servants. «و عباد الرّحمن... لایزنون»(2) And it considers avoiding this sin a condition for pledging allegiance (Bay'ah) to the Prophet. «اذا جائك ... و لا یزنین»(3)

Some of the social harms of zina are mentioned in a saying by Imam Reza عليه السلام: 1. Committing murder through abortion (sperm/fetus termination). 2. Disruption of the family and kinship system. 3. Neglect of child rearing. 4. Destruction of inheritance laws.(4)

  • Imam Ali عليه السلام considers abandoning zina a cause for strengthening the family and abandoning homosexuality a factor in preserving progeny.(5)
  • The Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله said: Zina has worldly and otherworldly harms: As for the world: the loss of human luminosity and beauty, premature death, and cutting off sustenance (Rizq). As for the Hereafter: helplessness during the Day of Judgment reckoning; Divine wrath, and eternal Hell.(6)
  • It is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: Whenever zina increases, sudden death also increases.(7)
  • Do not commit zina, so that your spouses are not also polluted by zina. As you treat others, so shall you be treated.(8)

Do not be heedless of the recompense of deeds Wheat grows from wheat, barley from barley.

  • There are four things, and whichever house contains any of them, that house is ruined and lacks blessing: 1- Treachery. 2- Theft. 3- Drinking alcohol. 4- Zina.(9)
  • Zina is one of the greatest major sins (Kabīrah).(10)
  • Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Whenever people commit four sins, they will be afflicted by four calamities: 1- When zina becomes widespread, earthquakes will occur. 2- Whenever people do not pay Zakat, livestock will perish. 3- Whenever the judgment of judicial officials is oppressive, rain will not fall. 4- When breach of covenant becomes widespread, polytheists will overcome Muslims.(11)

1) Isra, 32. 2) Furqan, 63 - 68. 3) Mumtahanah, 12. 4) Bihar, vol 79, p 24. 5) Nahj al-Balaghah, Hikmah 252. 6) Bihar, vol 77, p 58. 7) Bihar, vol 73, p 372. 8) Bihar, vol 76, p 27. 9) Bihar, vol 76, p 19. 10) Bihar, vol 76, p 19. 11) Bihar, vol 76, p 21.

Messages

1- Sexual freedom and illicit relationships are forbidden in Islam. «الزّانیة... فاجلدوا...»

2- The role of women in creating illicit relationships and preparing their preliminaries is greater than that of men, which is why the word Zāniyah (female fornicator/adulteress) precedes the word Zānī (male fornicator/adulterer). «الزّانیة و الزّانى» This is contrary to theft, where the role of men is greater, and thus the word Sāriq (male thief) precedes Sāriqah (female thief). «السّارق و السّارقة»(12)

3- Physical punishment of the fornicator/adulterer is necessary for their discipline and the preservation of public chastity. «فاجلدوا»

4- In illicit relationships, both parties are punished equally. (Except in cases where there is a specific ruling). «كل واحد»

5- The limits of punishment must be determined by Allah. «مأة جلدة»

6- In punishing criminals, compassion and being influenced by emotions are not permissible. «لا تأخذكم بهما رأفة»

7- Love and compassion must be within the framework of Sharia (religious law). «لا تأخذكم بهما رأفة»

8- Religion is not only prayer and fasting; the implementation of Divine limits (Hudūd) is also part of religion. «فى دین اللّه»

9- Firmness and decisiveness in implementing Divine limits are achieved through faith in the Origin (Allah) and the Return (Resurrection). «اِن كنتم تومنون بالله والیوم الآخر»

10- The punishment of the criminal must serve as a lesson for others. «و لیشهد عذابهما»

11- The fornicator/adulterer, in addition to physical torture, must also suffer spiritually/psychologically. «ولیشهد عذابهما طائفة...»

12- A group of believers must be present during the ceremony of implementing the Divine limit (Hadd). «من المؤمنین»

12) Ma'idah, 38.

Ayah

ٱلزَّانِى لَا يَنكِحُ إِلَّا زَانِيَةً أَوْ مُشْرِكَةً وَٱلزَّانِيَةُ لَا يَنكِحُهَآ إِلَّا زَانٍ أَوْ مُشْرِكٌ ۚ وَحُرِّمَ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۳)
The fornicator will not marry anyone but a fornicatress or an idolatress, and the fornicatress will be married by none except a fornicator or an idolater, and that is forbidden to the faithful. (3)

Key Points

This Ayah has the same atmosphere as the Ayah, "الخبیثات للخبیثین والخبیثون للخبیثات" (The wicked women are for the wicked men, and the wicked men are for the wicked women), which appears in Ayah 26 of this very Surah, and it expresses the principle that: birds of a feather flock together (literally: pigeon with pigeon, falcon with falcon). In this Ayah, the fornicator is placed alongside the polytheist. "زانیة او مشركة" (a female fornicator or a female polytheist). In addition to physical and social punishment, there must be other restrictions for the fornicator. It is mentioned in narrations that the ruling of this Ayah concerns those who are notorious for fornication. And if a fornicator repents, he can marry the person of his choice, just like others.(13) Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: In this Ayah, Allah has placed the fornicator opposite the believer, and this shows that the fornicator is not a believer at the time of fornication.(14)

(13) Tafsir al-Mizan; Kafi, Vol. 5, p. 354. (14) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 32.

Messages

1- In marriage, kufw (compatibility), meaning the spouses being equal and of the same status, is a principle. This is because impure men and women are not worthy of pure spouses. "الزّانى لا ینكح الاّ زانیه"

2- Corrupt individuals are deprived of certain rights in an Islamic society. "لا ینكح الاّ زانیة"

3- Believing men and women must choose pure and chaste spouses. "حرّم ذلك على المؤمنین"

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ ٱلْمُحْصَنَـٰتِ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَأْتُوا۟ بِأَرْبَعَةِ شُهَدَآءَ فَٱجْلِدُوهُمْ ثَمَـٰنِينَ جَلْدَةً وَلَا تَقْبَلُوا۟ لَهُمْ شَهَـٰدَةً أَبَدًا ۚ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْفَـٰسِقُونَ (٤)
As for those who accuse chaste women and do not bring four witnesses, strike them eighty lashes, and never accept any testimony from them after that, and they are transgressors, (4)

Key Points

Although the previous verses discussed the punishment for adulterers, proving adultery is not an easy or simple matter. Rather, four just witnesses must testify to its occurrence. If fewer than four people appear in court, each of them receives eighty lashes. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked: Why are two witnesses required to prove murder, but four witnesses are required to prove adultery? He replied: When murder is proven, one person is punished, but when adultery is proven, two people (the adulterous man and woman) are punished. (15) Among various accusations, the accusation of adultery has a specific prescribed limit (Hadd) and law, and the Quran explicitly emphasizes it. "ثمانین جلدة" (eighty lashes). The accusation is so serious that the difference in punishment between accusing someone of adultery and committing adultery itself is twenty lashes. "مأة جلدة" (one hundred lashes) - "ثمانین جلدة" (eighty lashes). The testimony and witness of a person who falsely accuses others of adultery are invalid. "لاتقبلوا لهم شهادة" (do not accept any testimony from them).

15) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq.

Messages

1- Slander is equivalent to shooting an arrow and attacking people's honor. "یرمون"

2- Accusing married and chaste women carries a harsher punishment. "المحصنات"

3- Defending the sanctity of chaste women is obligatory. "یرمون المحصنات... فاجلدوهم"

4- To protect people's honor, sometimes multiple individuals are lashed. "فاجلدوهم"

5- Slander has physical punishment, "فاجلدوهم" (then lash them); social punishment, "لا تقبلوا لهم شهادة ابدا" (never accept any testimony from them); and otherworldly punishment. "اولئك هم الفاسقون" (those are the transgressors).

6- Do not doubt the transgression (Fisq) of those who accuse chaste women. "هم الفاسقون" (they are the transgressors).

7- The condition for being a witness is justice (Adalah), and the testimony of a transgressor (Fasiq) is not accepted. "لا تقبلوا لهم شهادة... هم الفاسقون" (do not accept any testimony from them... they are the transgressors).

Ayah

إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ تَابُوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ وَأَصْلَحُوا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (٥)
excepting those who repent after that and reform, for Allah is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful. (5)

Key Points

We read in a Hadith: The repentance of one who has violated the honor of chaste women is that he retracts his statement publicly before the people. (16)

16) Kafi, Vol. 7, p. 241.

Messages

1- Repentance is the cause of renewed religious and social life. «الاّ الّذین تابوا... واصلحوا»

2- In the Divine religion, even sinners do not reach a dead end. «الاّ الّذین تابوا»

3- Repentance must be accompanied by reform, compensation for the mistake, and good deeds. «تابوا... واصلحوا»

4- Despair of Divine forgiveness and mercy is unwarranted. «اِنّ اللّه غفور رحیم»

5- The openness of the path of repentance for everyone is a sign of Divine mercy. «رحیم»

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ أَزْوَٰجَهُمْ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُمْ شُهَدَآءُ إِلَّآ أَنفُسُهُمْ فَشَهَـٰدَةُ أَحَدِهِمْ أَرْبَعُ شَهَـٰدَٰتٍۭ بِٱللَّهِ ۙ إِنَّهُۥ لَمِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ (٦)
As for those who accuse their wives [of adultery], but have no witnesses except themselves, the testimony of such a man shall be a fourfold testimony [sworn] by Allah that he is indeed stating the truth, (6)
وَٱلْخَـٰمِسَةُ أَنَّ لَعْنَتَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ إِن كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰذِبِينَ (۷)
and a fifth [oath] that Allah’s wrath shall be upon him if he were lying. (7)
وَيَدْرَؤُا۟ عَنْهَا ٱلْعَذَابَ أَن تَشْهَدَ أَرْبَعَ شَهَـٰدَٰتٍۭ بِٱللَّهِ ۙ إِنَّهُۥ لَمِنَ ٱلْكَـٰذِبِينَ (۸)
The punishment shall be averted from her by her testifying with four oaths [sworn] by Allah that he is indeed lying, (8)
وَٱلْخَـٰمِسَةَ أَنَّ غَضَبَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْهَآ إِن كَانَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ (۹)
and a fifth [oath] that Allah’s wrath shall be upon her if he were stating the truth. (9)
وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُۥ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ تَوَّابٌ حَكِيمٌ (۱٠)
Were it not for Allah’s grace and His mercy upon you, and that Allah is all-clement, all-wise.... (10)

Key Points

A man named "Hilal ibn Umayyah" entered upon the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله in distress and said: I saw my wife committing adultery (zina). By God, I speak the truth. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was troubled. His Companions were also agitated because this man had both witnessed his honor (wife) in such a state and would also be flogged, as he had no witness to prove his claim. This Ayah was revealed and stated: If a man accuses his wife of adultery and has no witness, instead of bringing four witnesses, he should say four times: «اُشهد باللّه انّى لمن الصادقین» (I call Allah to witness that I am surely among the truthful). And to strengthen and confirm his claim, he should also say this phrase once: «لعنة اللّه علىّ ان كنتُ من الكاذبین» (The curse of Allah be upon me if I am among the liars).

Of course, the wife, like her husband, has the right to defend herself, because it is possible for a man, through four false oaths, to condemn his wife to stoning and permanently ruin her reputation and that of her relatives. Islam, which supports the rights of all, has permitted the wife, in order to prevent this occurrence, to swear an oath four times to prove her chastity and save herself from stoning, saying: «اُشهد باللّه انّه لَمِن الكاذِبین» (I call Allah to witness that my husband is surely among the liars). And for greater assurance, she should say: «غَضِب اللّه علىّ اِن كان من الصادقین» (The wrath and anger of Allah be upon me if my husband is among the truthful).

In the man's fifth statement, it was "the curse of Allah," and in the woman's fifth statement, it was "the wrath of Allah," which is more severe than the curse, because the sensitivity of the person who has been accused is greater, and to save herself from stoning, she is willing to endure the wrath of Allah. In any case, if these ten statements (five from the man and five from the woman) are uttered in the presence of an Islamic judge or ruler, this act is technically called "Li'an" (mutual cursing). Of course, specific jurisprudential and legal issues are involved in this matter. The judge must admonish the husband and wife and try to prevent the Li'an from taking place, and if execution is necessary, he should take them to sacred places like the mosque and seat them facing the Qibla, and then the Li'an ceremony should be performed.

With the execution of Li'an, the swearing of oaths by the husband and wife, and the utterance of the mentioned phrases, Islam has established laws that must be followed: 1- This man and woman are permanently separated from each other without a formal divorce (sighah of talaq) and have no right to reconcile or remarry. 2- The prescribed punishment (Hadd) is lifted from both; meaning the man is not given eighty lashes, and the woman is not stoned.

Establishing four witnesses or four oaths, accompanied by a curse and wrath, for the proof of adultery (zina), is a sign that Allah, through His grace and mercy via such rulings, prevents the disgrace of individuals; otherwise, families would be disgraced and tribes and relatives would be ruined every day.

Messages

1- Preserving reputation (honor) is important in Islam. «أربع شهادات باللّه» (The law of four oaths and one self-curse is to control people from disgracing one another).

2- In proving the adultery (zina) of a spouse, four oaths and one curse are accepted instead of four witnesses. «اِن لم یكن لهم شهداء... فَشَهادةُ أحدهم أربع شهادات»

3- Islam is the protector of women's rights. The wife can also repel an accusation that the husband has established with four oaths, by swearing four oaths herself. «أربع شهادات... أربع شهادات»

4- To prevent the collapse of the family structure, Islam has made the utterance of a fifth statement obligatory upon each party, in addition to calling Allah to witness four times. «والخامسة»

5- Self-imprecation (cursing oneself) is permissible to remove an accusation. «غضب اللّه علیها»

6- The implementation of Allah's laws and commands is for the benefit of the people themselves. «و لولا فضل اللّه علیكم»

7- Allah's acceptance of repentance is accompanied by expediency and wisdom. «تَوّابٌ حكیم»

8- Islamic penal laws are rulings derived from Divine wisdom. «حكیم»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ جَآءُو بِٱلْإِفْكِ عُصْبَةٌ مِّنكُمْ ۚ لَا تَحْسَبُوهُ شَرًّا لَّكُم ۖ بَلْ هُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۚ لِكُلِّ ٱمْرِئٍ مِّنْهُم مَّا ٱكْتَسَبَ مِنَ ٱلْإِثْمِ ۚ وَٱلَّذِى تَوَلَّىٰ كِبْرَهُۥ مِنْهُمْ لَهُۥ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ (۱۱)
Indeed those who initiated the calumny are a group from among yourselves. Do not suppose it is bad thing for you. No, it is for your good. Each man among them bears [the onus for] his share in the sin, and as for him who assumed its major burden from among them, there is a great punishment for him. (11)

Key Points

The word al-Ifk (اِفك) linguistically means inclining from truth (haqq) toward falsehood (batil). Slander (or accusation) is also a type of covering up the truth and presenting falsehood. Some commentators regarding the context of the revelation (shān nuzūl) of this Ayah have said: The Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله would take one of his wives with him on every journey by drawing lots. In the Battle of Banu Mustaliq, he took 'A'ishah. When the battle ended and the people were returning to Medina, 'A'ishah fell behind the caravan, either for purification or to find the beads of her lost necklace. One of the Companions, who had also been separated from the caravan, brought 'A'ishah back to the army. Some individuals leveled an unjust accusation against 'A'ishah and that Companion. This accusation reached the people, the Prophet became distressed, and 'A'ishah went to her father's house, until this Ayah was revealed. (17)

Another group of commentators, especially the author of the commentary Atayab al-Bayan, using multiple documented narrations, relate that this Ayah concerns Mariyah al-Qibtiyyah (another wife of the Messenger of Allah), to whom Allah granted a son named Ibrahim, but who passed away after 18 months. 'A'ishah and Hafsah said: O Messenger of Allah! Why are you weeping? Ibrahim was not your son; rather, someone other than you had intercourse with Mariyah, and Ibrahim was born. It was here that this Ayah was revealed. Allamah Ja'far Murtada also confirms this account in his valuable book, Hadith al-Ifk.

Questions are raised regarding both contexts of revelation, but since our goal in this commentary is to derive messages from the text of the Ayahs, we will not engage in an extensive discussion about the contexts of revelation. Several sins are inherent in the incident of al-Ifk: lying, suspicion of a believer, harming a believer (īdha' al-mu'min), slander, insult, distressing the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, weakening the Household of the Message (Ahl al-Risalah), and rumor-mongering.

(17) Tafsir Ibn Kathir.

Messages

1- Immoral individuals (fāsiqūn) have no hesitation in attributing fornication (or adultery), even to the Prophet's wife. «جاءو بالاِفك»

2- Sometimes rumor-mongers spread rumors with prior organization and intent to conspire. «عُصبة» ('Usbah means an organized and zealous group pursuing a specific goal.)

3- Be watchful of the rumor-mongers among yourselves. «منكم»

4- Sometimes there are benefits in bitter occurrences (such as the exposure of the hypocrites, the vigilance of the people, and Divine aid for the oppressed). «لاتحسبوه شرّاً لكم»

5- Giving hope to believers in the face of conspiracies and accusations by ill-wishers is necessary. «بل هو خیرٌ»

6- Divine punishments are just and proportionate to our deeds. «لكلّ امرىٍ منهم ما اكتسب»

7- In collective sins, each individual is guilty according to his share. «لكلّ امرىٍ منهم ما اكتسب من الاثم»

8- A person is guilty and culpable when he commits a sin knowingly and intentionally. «اِكتسب من الاثم»

9- The sin of every person affects only himself. «لكلّ امرىٍ منهم مَا اكتسب من الاثم»

10- The punishment for the one who played the principal role in committing the crime is greater. «والّذى تَولّى كِبره... له عذابٌ عظیم»

Ayahs

لَّوْلَآ إِذْ سَمِعْتُمُوهُ ظَنَّ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتُ بِأَنفُسِهِمْ خَيْرًا وَقَالُوا۟ هَـٰذَآ إِفْكٌ مُّبِينٌ (۱۲)
When you [first] heard about it, why did not the faithful, men and women, think well of their folks, and say, ‘This is an obvious calumny’? (12)
لَّوْلَا جَآءُو عَلَيْهِ بِأَرْبَعَةِ شُهَدَآءَ ۚ فَإِذْ لَمْ يَأْتُوا۟ بِٱلشُّهَدَآءِ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ هُمُ ٱلْكَـٰذِبُونَ (۱۳)
Why did they not bring four witnesses to it? So when they could not bring the witnesses, they are liars in Allah’s sight. (13)

Key Points

In this Ayah, Allah reprimands the Muslims for their suspicion regarding the Prophet's wife and their vulnerability to rumors. The Quran sometimes uses the word "you" (or "yourselves") instead of the word "others" to tell the Muslims that all of you are one. For example, instead of saying: Do not mock or taunt others, He says: «لا تَلمِزوا اَنفسكم»(18) Do not taunt yourselves. And instead of saying: When you enter a house, greet others, He says: «فسَلّموا على اَنفسِكم»(19) Greet yourselves. In this Ayah too, instead of saying: Have good assumptions about others, He says: «ظنّ... بأنفسهم خیراً» Have good assumptions about yourselves.

In the Noble Quran, the word «لولا» (Lola) has been used in multiple instances for criticism and reprimand. We mention a few cases here: 1- «لولا یَنهاهم الرّبّانیّون»(20) Why do the scholars not forbid evil? 2- «لولا اذ سَمعتُموه»(21) Why do you accept everything you hear? 3- «لولا جاءوا علیه باَربعة شُهداء»(22) Why do they not bring four witnesses for their wrongful statement? 4- «لولا تَستَغفرون اللّه»(23) Why do you not seek forgiveness from Allah? 5- «فلولا اذ جاءهم بأسُنا تَضرّعوا»(24) Why did they not humble themselves when Our punishment reached them? 6- «فلولا تَشكرون»(25) Why are you not grateful? 7- «فلولا تصدّقون»(26) Why do you not affirm (the truth)? 8- «فلولا تذكّرون»(27) Why do you not take heed? 9- «فلولا نَفَر من كلّ فِرقَة مِنهم طائفة لِیَتفقّهوا فِى الدّین»(28) Why does not a group from every division among them migrate to gain understanding in the religion?

18) Al-Hujurat, 11. 19) An-Noor, 61. 20) Al-Ma'idah, 63. 21) An-Noor, 12. 22) An-Noor, 13. 23) An-Naml, 46. 24) Al-An'am, 43. 25) Al-Waqi'ah, 70. 26) Al-Waqi'ah, 57. 27) Al-Waqi'ah, 62. 28) At-Tawbah, 122.

Messages

1- Spreading and fueling rumors is forbidden. («لولا» is a sign of reprimand against suspicion and the spreading of rumors.)

2- The spirit of good assumption (حسن ظن) must prevail in the Islamic society. «ظَنّ المؤمنون و المؤمنات بأنفسهم خَیراً» (Good assumption is one of the effects of faith.)

3- Believers are responsible for the honor of an "individual." «المؤمنون و المؤمنات»

4- The children of Adam are members of one another. Slander against any member of the Islamic society is like slander against the other members. «بأنفسهم خَیراً»

5- The primary principle regarding a Muslim's action is soundness (innocence), unless his crime is proven by evidence. «ظَنّ المؤمنون والمؤمنات بأنفسِهم خَیراً»

6- Protecting the sanctity of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and his family is obligatory upon every believer. «لولا... قالوا هذا إفك مبین»

7- Silence is forbidden in the face of wrongful rumors concerning believers. «لولا... قالوا هذا إفك مبین»

8- A simple-minded, gullible, and frivolous society is reprimanded. «لولاجاءو علیه باَربعة»

9- If an accusation is not proven, the speaker is considered a liar. «هم الكاذبون»

Ayah

وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُۥ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ لَمَسَّكُمْ فِى مَآ أَفَضْتُمْ فِيهِ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ (۱٤)
Were it not for Allah’s grace and His mercy upon you in this world and the Hereafter, there would have befallen you a great punishment for what you ventured into, (14)

Key Points

The word afadtum (أفَضتم) derives from ifādah (اِفاضة) and is used when a matter is discussed extensively and occupies everyone. This indicates that the slander against the Prophet's wife had become the topic of the day, and everyone was preoccupied with it.

Messages

1- The Islamic Ummah (community) is under the protection and grace of Allah. «و لولافضل اللّه...»

2- The mercy and grace of Allah are due to His favor, not our deservingness. «فضل اللّه علیكم و رحمته»

3- Rumors are like a whirlpool in which some people drown. «اَفَضتم فیه»

4- The general public is susceptible to accepting rumors and slanders and to the descent of calamities. «و لولا... لمسّكم عذاب عظیم

Ayah

إِذْ تَلَقَّوْنَهُۥ بِأَلْسِنَتِكُمْ وَتَقُولُونَ بِأَفْوَاهِكُم مَّا لَيْسَ لَكُم بِهِۦ عِلْمٌ وَتَحْسَبُونَهُۥ هَيِّنًا وَهُوَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ عَظِيمٌ (۱٥)
when you were receiving it on your tongues, and were mouthing something of which you had no knowledge, supposing it to be a light matter, while it was a grave [matter] with Allah. (15)

Key Points

The tongue is the only organ that, throughout a person's life, neither feels pain nor tires from speaking. Its shape and size are small, but its offense and sin are great. How many are the disbelievers who become Muslim and pure by uttering a single sentence (testifying to the Oneness of Allah), and how many are the Muslims who become disbelievers (kafir) and impure (najis) by uttering disbelief in God or denying the rulings of the religion. On the one hand, the tongue can create purity through truthfulness, remembrance (dhikr), supplication (du'a), and advising others; and on the other hand, it is capable of creating resentment by stinging this person and that. The tongue is the key to intellect, the lamp of knowledge, and the simplest and cheapest means of transferring sciences and experiences. Scholars of ethics have written extensively about the tongue.

Messages

1- Accepting what is on people's tongues without investigation is condemned. «تلقونه باَلسِنتكم»

2- Speech must be based on knowledge. «تقولون باَفواهِكم ما لیس لكم به علم»

3- We should not take lightly the act of questioning people's honor (reputation). «وتَحسِبونَه هَیّناً»

4- Transmitting accusations and rumors is among the major sins (kabirah). «و هو عنداللّه عظیم»

5- Allah determines the smallness or greatness of a sin. «عنداللّه عظیم»

6- Not all human calculations are realistic. «تَحسِبونه هَیّناً و هو عنداللّه عظیم»

Ayah

وَلَوْلَآ إِذْ سَمِعْتُمُوهُ قُلْتُم مَّا يَكُونُ لَنَآ أَن نَّتَكَلَّمَ بِهَـٰذَا سُبْحَـٰنَكَ هَـٰذَا بُهْتَـٰنٌ عَظِيمٌ (۱٦)
And why did you not, when you heard it, say, ‘It is not for us to say such a thing. [O Allah!] You are immaculate! This is a monstrous calumny!’ (16)

Key Points

The word 'aẓīm (great/mighty) appears in three consecutive verses: once as "عذابٌ عظیم" (a mighty punishment) and twice (in two different expressions) as "بهتانٌ عظیم" (a mighty slander), indicating that a great sin incurs a great penalty.

Messages

1- Enjoining the good and forbidding the evil (nahy ‘an al-munkar) is obligatory. (و لولا اذ سَمعتموه قلتم) (Speaking up to defend the honor of a Muslim is obligatory.)

2- Man is responsible for what he hears. (لولا اذسمعتموه)

3- When encountering important matters, say "سبحان اللّه" (Glory be to Allah). (سبحانك)

4- In the sight of Allah, the world is small, but slandering a Muslim is great. (بهتانٌ عظیم)

Ayahs

يَعِظُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ أَن تَعُودُوا۟ لِمِثْلِهِۦٓ أَبَدًا إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۱۷)
Allah advises you lest you should ever repeat the like of it, should you be faithful. (17)
وَيُبَيِّنُ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ (۱۸)
Allah clarifies the signs for you, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (18)

Messages

1- People need admonition, and Allah is the best Admonisher. «یَعِظُكم اللّه»

2- Faith is the condition for the effectiveness of the admonition. «یَعِظُكم... اِن كنتم مؤمنین»

3- To preserve people's honor and prevent suspicion (su' al-ẓann), others must be admonished. «یَعِظُكم اللّه أن تَعودوا لمثله»

4- Fueling suspicion and slander causes the erasure of faith. «یَعِظُكم... ان كنتم مؤمنین»

5- Regret over the past is not enough; the sin must not be repeated. «تَعودوا لمثله»

6- Not repeating the transgression is a sign of faith. «تَعودوا لمثله اِن كنتم مؤمنین»

7- Do not slander, and assume the soundness of a Muslim's action. «أن تَعودوا لمثله»

8- Admonish sinners to repent from sin. «یَعِظُكم اللّه أن تَعودوا لمثله»

9- The source of Islamic commands is Divine knowledge and wisdom. «علیمٌ حكیم»

10- Divine rulings are based on Divine knowledge and wisdom. «و یُبیّن اللّه... علیمٌ حكیم»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يُحِبُّونَ أَن تَشِيعَ ٱلْفَـٰحِشَةُ فِى ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (۱۹)
Indeed those who want indecency to spread among the faithful—there is a painful punishment for them in the world and the Hereafter, and Allah knows and you do not know. (19)
وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُۥ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ رَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۲٠)
Were it not for Allah’s grace and His mercy upon you, and that Allah is all-kind, all-merciful… (20)

Key Points

The propagation of indecency (fāḥishah) is sometimes done through speech and pen, exposing the ugly deeds of people, and sometimes by encouraging others to sin and providing them with the means to commit sin. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: "Whoever recounts to others what he hears about a believer is among the instances covered by this Ayah."(29) We read in a tradition (hadith): "Whoever spreads an ugly deed is like the one who committed it."(30)

29) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq. 30) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq.

Messages

1- We are responsible for our desires. «یُحبّون... لهم عذاب»

2- Love for sin is the prelude to sin. In the path of forbidding evil (Nahy 'an al-Munkar), we must eradicate the love for evil. «یُحبّون ...»

3- Even loving certain sins is a major sin (kabīrah), such as destroying the reputation (honor) of a believer. «یُحبّون... عذابٌ الیم»

4- The Islamic system must punish those who seek to propagate indecency (fāḥishah). «عذابٌ الیمٌ فى الدّنیا»

5- Perhaps the source of some worldly punishments that befall us is the destruction of others' reputations. «و انتم لا تعلمون»

Ayah

۞ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَتَّبِعُوا۟ خُطُوَٰتِ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ ۚ وَمَن يَتَّبِعْ خُطُوَٰتِ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ فَإِنَّهُۥ يَأْمُرُ بِٱلْفَحْشَآءِ وَٱلْمُنكَرِ ۚ وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُۥ مَا زَكَىٰ مِنكُم مِّنْ أَحَدٍ أَبَدًا وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُزَكِّى مَن يَشَآءُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۲۱)
O you who have faith! Do not follow in Satan’s steps. Whoever follows in Satan’s steps [should know that] he indeed prompts [you to commit] indecent and wrongful acts. Were it not for Allah’s grace and His mercy upon you, not one of you would ever become pure. But Allah purifies whomever He wishes, and Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing. (21)

Key Points

In verses 14, 20, and 21, the favor (faḍl) and mercy (raḥmah) of Allah are mentioned three times to indicate that if His grace had not been present, it would be unknown what your situation would be! Khutuwāt (خُطوات) is the plural of khutwah (خُطوة), meaning 'step' or 'pace'. The Quran uses the phrase khutuwāt ash-Shayṭān (خطوات الشیطان) regarding the method by which Satan misleads, indicating that Satan draws man toward committing sins step by step. The phrase khutuwāt ash-Shayṭān (خُطوات الشیطان) is mentioned several times in the Quran. In one place, He says: «اُدخلوا فى السِلم كافّة و لاتَتّبعوا خُطوات الشیطان»(31) (Enter into peace and reconciliation completely, and do not follow the steps of Satan.) And in another place, He says: «كُلوا ممّا فى الارض حلالاً طیّبا و لا تَتّبعوا خُطوات الشیطان»(32) (Eat of what is lawful and good on the earth, and be mindful of the steps of Satan and do not follow them.) In this verse, concerning the spread of indecency (fāḥishah) regarding the believers, He also says: Do not follow the steps of Satan. Examples of Satan's steps include: befriending corrupt individuals, participating in their gatherings, committing minor sins, followed by major sins, and ultimately, hardness of heart (qasāwat al-qalb), a bad outcome, and a dreadful end.

31) Baqarah, 208. 32) Baqarah, 168.

Messages

1- We should not be arrogant about our faith; continuous attention to repeated warnings is necessary. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا لا تَتّبعوا»

2- Satan strives to mislead the believers. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا لا تَتّبعوا خُطوات الشیطان»

3- Satan's policy is gradual, step-by-step infiltration. «خطوات الشیطان» (We must be careful from the very first step.)

4- Spreading indecency (fāḥishah) is one of Satan's steps. (Considering the previous verse) «لا تَتّبعوا خُطوات الشیطان»

5- We should speak to people with evidence. When Allah commands: Do not follow Satan, He mentions the reason: because he commands sin. «فاِنّه یأمر بالفحشاء...»

6- Satan initially whispers (tempts), and then commands anyone who follows his whispers. «و مَن یتّبع خُطوات... فاِنّه یَأمر...»

7- Satan's work is in contradiction with prayer (Salat). Satan commands man to sin, «فاِنّه یأمر بالفحشاء و المنكر» (But prayer restrains man from sin. «انّ الصلوة تنهى عن الفحشاء و المنكر...»(33))

8- Purification (Tazkiyah) is impossible without Divine success (Tawfīq). «ولولا ... ما زَكى ...»

9- Man is always in need of Allah's grace. (Repetition of the phrase «لولا فضل اللّه» in this verse and the preceding verses)

10- Deception and indecency exist in the path of human life, but the path of repentance (Tawbah) and salvation is also open. «و لولا فضل اللّه»

11- Even the friends of Allah (Awliyā') need Divine success. «ما زَكى منكم من اَحد»

12- The Wise Allah sends down goodness and felicity according to the merit and capacity of individuals. «یزكّى من یشاء»

13- The knowledge that Allah sees and hears everything is the best deterrent against indecency (fāḥishah) and evil deeds (munkarāt). «واللّه سمیعٌ علیم»

33) 'Ankabūt, 45.

Ayah

وَلَا يَأْتَلِ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْفَضْلِ مِنكُمْ وَٱلسَّعَةِ أَن يُؤْتُوٓا۟ أُو۟لِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَٱلْمَسَـٰكِينَ وَٱلْمُهَـٰجِرِينَ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَلْيَعْفُوا۟ وَلْيَصْفَحُوٓا۟ ۗ أَلَا تُحِبُّونَ أَن يَغْفِرَ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۲۲)
The well-off and the opulent among you should not vow that they will not give [any longer] to the relatives and the needy, and to those who have migrated in the way of Allah, and let them excuse and forbear. Do you not love that Allah should forgive you? Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (22)

Key Points

The word "Ya'tal" (یأتل) is derived from the root "alw" (اَلْو), meaning to fall short or neglect, or from "alā" (اَلى), meaning to swear an oath, similar to "Īlā’" (ایلاء) in jurisprudential discussions. (34) "‘Afw" (عفو) means forgiveness, and "Safh" (صَفح) means overlooking, which is a higher stage than ‘Afw. Some have said that "Safh" is the forgiveness in which there is no blame. In the preceding verses, those who spread slander were severely criticized. Some companions swore that they would never give charity (infaq) to the slanderers. This verse states: Do not deprive the needy due to past mistakes. (Occasion of Revelation)

34) Mufradāt al-Rāghib.

Messages

1- The affluent are responsible towards the deprived. «و لا یَأتل اولوا الفضل...»

2- More important than giving charity (infaq) is its continuity, not tiring of it, and not falling short in it. «و لا یَأتل »

3- In giving charity, utilize both financial abundance and the capabilities of others. «اولوا الفضل ... والسّعة»

4- Relatives have priority in providing aid. «اولى القُربى»

5- Be ambitious in traversing the stages of perfection and do not settle for the minimum. First, the word "‘Afw" (forgiveness) is mentioned, and at the higher stage, "Safh" (overlooking) is mentioned. «ولْیَعفوا ولیَصفحوا»

6- Whoever forgives others is closer to Divine forgiveness. (Forgiveness and pardon are means of attracting Allah’s forgiveness). «الا تحبّون أن یغفر اللّه لكم»

7- The goal of forgiving and pardoning others should be to attain Allah’s forgiveness, and nothing else. «الا تُحبّون أن یَغفر اللّه لكم»

8- In the method of propagation (Tabligh), we should utilize people's feelings and emotions. «الا تحبون»

9- Religious leaders must encourage people towards charity (infaq), forgiveness, and pardon. (The entire Ayah)

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ ٱلْمُحْصَنَـٰتِ ٱلْغَـٰفِلَـٰتِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ لُعِنُوا۟ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ (۲۳)
Indeed those who accuse chaste and unwary faithful women shall be cursed in this world and the Hereafter, and there shall be a great punishment for them (23)

Key Points

Although in the Quran, disbelievers, covenant-breakers, murderers, supporters of tyranny, hypocrites, corruptors, polytheists, arrogant ones, oppressors, concealers of the truth, and liars have been cursed, the curse of this world and the Hereafter, accompanied by a great punishment, is specifically reserved for those who ruin the reputation of pure individuals. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام considered this verse evidence that falsely accusing chaste and virtuous women is among the major sins. (35)

35) Bihar, Vol. 76, p. 9.

Messages

1- Women are more vulnerable; those who cause harm must be warned. «یرمون المحصنات»

2- The purer a person is, the more dangerous the slander against them. «المحصناتِ الغافلات المؤمنات»

3- Slander, in addition to the punishment of the Hereafter, also carries a penalty in this world. «لُعِنوا فى الدّنیا» (Perhaps the example of the curse in this world is the execution of the prescribed punishment (hadd) and the rejection of his testimony as a transgressor.)

4- Protecting the rights of chaste women is among the programs of the Quran. «یَرمون المحصنات... لُعِنوا»

Ayah

يَوْمَ تَشْهَدُ عَلَيْهِمْ أَلْسِنَتُهُمْ وَأَيْدِيهِمْ وَأَرْجُلُهُم بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۲٤)
on the day when witness shall be given against them by their tongues, their hands, and their feet concerning what they used to do. (24)

Key Points

The issue of the body parts testifying on the Day of Judgment has been raised repeatedly in the Quran. In Surah Fussilat, we read: «حتّى اذا ما جاءوها شَهِد علیهم سَمعُهم و ابصارُهم و جُلودُهم بما كانوا یعملون»(36) When they reach Hellfire, their ears, eyes, and skin will testify to the sins they committed. In Surah Ya Sin, we read: «ألیوم نَختِمُ على أفواهِهم و تُكلِّمُنا ایدیهِم و تَشهَد أرجُلُهم بما كانوا یَكسِبون»(37) On that Day, We shall seal the mouths of the disbelievers, and their hands will speak to Us, and their feet will testify to what they used to earn. Since testimony requires bearing witness concerning another entity, it becomes clear that the essence and personality of the human being are separate from his body parts that testify against him. This is because the witness and the testified-against must be distinct from one another.

36) Fussilat, 20. 37) Ya Sin, 65.

Messages

1- In the court of the Day of Judgment, man does not even own his own body parts. «تَشهدُ علیهم السنتهم و ایدیهم و ارجلهم»

2- Giving testimony requires perception and awareness. Therefore, the body parts perceive what they do, even if we do not understand it. «تَشهَدُ علیهم السنتهم و ایدیهم و ارجلهم»

3- On the Day of Judgment, testimony will be brought concerning all speech and actions. «بما كانوا یعملون»

Ayah

يَوْمَئِذٍ يُوَفِّيهِمُ ٱللَّهُ دِينَهُمُ ٱلْحَقَّ وَيَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلْحَقُّ ٱلْمُبِينُ (۲٥)
On that day, Allah will pay them in full their due recompense, and they shall know that Allah is the Manifest Reality. (25)

Key Points

The word dīn (دين) in the Ayah is either meant as recompense (jazā’) or as Divine Law (Sharī‘ah). Therefore, the meaning of the Ayah is either that on the Day of Resurrection (Qiyāmah), Allah will give people their full recompense, or that the true Divine Law will be presented on that Day, and all those who fabricated imaginary schools of thought will realize that Allah is the manifest and true Deity. (38) In this world, due to existing limitations, full punishment is not administered. For example, if a pilot bombs a city and kills thousands of people, how can we punish him fully in this world? But on the Day of Resurrection, there are no limitations, and the criminal will be burned in Hell (Dūzakh) repeatedly and brought back to life.

(38) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- It is only on the Day of Resurrection that punishments are administered fully. «یومئذ یُوَفّیهم»

2- Divine punishments on the Day of Resurrection are based on testimony, witness, and truth. «تشهد علیهم... یُوَفّیهم اللّه»

3- Allah does not diminish anyone's punishment or reward. «یُوَفّیهم»

4- On the Day of Resurrection, when the veils of heedlessness, arrogance, ignorance, false propaganda, and desires are removed, people will understand the truth. «و یعلمون... هو الحقّ»

Ayah

ٱلْخَبِيثَـٰتُ لِلْخَبِيثِينَ وَٱلْخَبِيثُونَ لِلْخَبِيثَـٰتِ ۖ وَٱلطَّيِّبَـٰتُ لِلطَّيِّبِينَ وَٱلطَّيِّبُونَ لِلطَّيِّبَـٰتِ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ مُبَرَّءُونَ مِمَّا يَقُولُونَ ۖ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ (۲٦)
Vicious women are for vicious men, and vicious men for vicious women. Good women are for good men, and good men for good women. These are absolved of what they say [about them]. For them is forgiveness and a noble provision. (26)

Key Points

This Ayah presents a general principle: that in choosing a spouse, one must be careful and base the choice on faith and chastity, not beauty or wealth. Therefore, the meaning of the Ayah is not that if a man or woman is good, their spouse must also be good and thus included in forgiveness (maghfirah) and noble provision (rizq karim), because the Quran considers the criteria for entering Paradise to be faith, piety (taqwa), and righteous deeds (‘amal salih). Hence, even though Prophets Noah (عليه السلام) and Lot (عليه السلام) were tayyib (pure), their wives were among the khabithat (impure) and destined for Hell. The word tayyib means pleasing and desirable. In the Quran, it is used to describe wealth, progeny, children, speech, city, spouse, food and provision, dwelling, tree, and greeting. Its opposite is the word khabith, which is also used as a description for wealth, human beings, spouse, speech, action, and tree.

This Ayah can be interpreted in several ways:

A: Considering the preceding verses concerning the slander against chaste women and the incident of Ifk (the great lie), and considering the phrase: «مبرّئون ممّا یقولون» (they are innocent of what they say), the meaning of the Ayah is that impure words, such as slander and accusation, are fitting for impure people, and pure words are fitting for pure people. Wicked people speak foul words, and pure people speak pure words.

B: The Ayah might intend to state a legal ruling (hukm shar‘i) that the marriage of the pure with the impure is forbidden. Similar to the first Ayah of this very Surah, which states: «الزّانى لایَنكح الاّ زانیة» (The fornicator shall not marry except a fornicatress), just as a narration from Imam Baqir (عليه السلام) confirms this meaning. (39)

C: The Ayah might refer to intellectual, ideological, and moral compatibility, or what is called being of equal status (ham-sha’n) and equal standing (ham-kufw) in a spouse. That is, everyone naturally seeks out those who share their views.

Those of fire attract those of fire, Those of light seek those of light.

In the Quran, several descriptions are mentioned for provision (rizq), including: «رزقاً حسنا» (40), a good and pure provision. «رزقٌ كریم» (41), a noble and esteemed provision. «و كلوا ممّا رَزَقكم اللّه حلالاً طیّبا» (42), Eat of what Allah has provided for you, lawful and pure. «یَرزُقه من حیث لایَحتسب» (43), He provides for him from where He does not expect. «یُرزَقون فیها بغیر حساب» (44) In Paradise, they will be provided for without measure.

39) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan. 40) An-Nahl, 67. 41) Al-Anfal, 4. 42) Al-Ma’idah, 88. 43) At-Talaq, 3.

Messages

1- The nature of wicked individuals is such that they gravitate towards wickedness, contrary to pure-natured individuals who gravitate towards goodness. «الخبیثیات للخبیثین... الطیّبات للطّیّبین»

2- Benefiting from blessings and pure things (tayyibat) is the right of the people of faith. «والطّیّبات للطّیبّین»

3- The preservation of pure generations is among the recommendations of the Quran. «والطیّبات للطّیبّین»

4- Supporting the chaste is necessary. «اولئك مبرؤن ممّا یقولون»

5- Spirituality precedes materialism. (Wherever the phrase «رزق كریم» (noble provision) appears in the Quran, it is preceded by the phrase «لهم مغفرة» (for them is forgiveness).)

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَدْخُلُوا۟ بُيُوتًا غَيْرَ بُيُوتِكُمْ حَتَّىٰ تَسْتَأْنِسُوا۟ وَتُسَلِّمُوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهَا ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ (۲۷)
O you who have faith! Do not enter houses other than your own until you have announced [your arrival] and greeted their occupants. That is better for you. Maybe you will take admonition. (27)

Key Points

The meaning of isti'nās (اِستیناس) is seeking permission and announcing entry, which, according to narrations, can be done by mentioning Allah or by giving salutations (salām) and the like. (45) A person asked the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: Should I seek permission to enter my mother's house? He replied: Yes. The person said: There is no one in the house but her, and she has no servant except me! The Prophet said: Do you like to see your mother without proper clothing? He said no. He said: Then seek permission. In another narration, the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله forbade men from entering the houses of women whose husbands were absent. (46) We read in narrations: Seeking permission to enter the houses of others should be done three times so that the occupants of the house have the opportunity to compose themselves. And for saving a drowning person, an oppressed person, or someone involved in a fire, permission is not necessary. (47) Whenever the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله wanted to enter a house, when seeking permission, He would not stand directly facing the door so that His blessed gaze would not fall inside the house. (48)

44) Ghafir, 40. 45) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 46) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 47) Tafsir Kabir Fakhr Razi. 48) Tafasir Kabir wa Fi Zilal.

Messages

1- The people of faith must take instructions from Divine Revelation in matters of life. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا»

2- Security and freedom must be preserved for the occupants of the house. «لا تَدخلوا» (Unannounced entry into the privacy of others is forbidden.)

3- Avoid situations that lead to suspicion (or accusation). «لا تَدخلوا... ذلكم خیر»

4- An open door is not proof of permission to enter. «لا تَدخلوا»

5- The ownership rights of individuals must be respected. «لا تَدخلوا»

6- The interaction of believers with one another must be accompanied by affection. «تَستأنسوا و تُسلّموا»

7- Avoid any action that leads to the violation of the boundaries of modesty (ḥayā') and hijab. «تستأنسوا»

8- The person entering must give salām (greetings), whoever he may be. «تسلّموا على اهلها» (Giving salām is not necessarily required from the younger to the older.)

9- Observing etiquette and the rights of others is the source of human felicity. «ذلكم خیرلكم»

10- We should not forget the Divine commands, but keep them in mind. «لعلّكم تَذكّرون»

11- Religious manners and ethics are the very call of the innate nature (fiṭrah), which has been neglected and must be awakened through remembrance (tadhakkur). «تَذكّرون»

Ayah

فَإِن لَّمْ تَجِدُوا۟ فِيهَآ أَحَدًا فَلَا تَدْخُلُوهَا حَتَّىٰ يُؤْذَنَ لَكُمْ ۖ وَإِن قِيلَ لَكُمُ ٱرْجِعُوا۟ فَٱرْجِعُوا۟ ۖ هُوَ أَزْكَىٰ لَكُمْ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ عَلِيمٌ (۲۸)
But if you do not find anyone in them, do not enter them until you are given permission. And if you are told: ‘Turn back,’ then do turn back. That is more decent for you. Allah knows best what you do. (28)

Messages

1- Entering the homes of others without the permission of the owner is forbidden (Haram). «فلاتَدخلوا»

2- Entry requires definitive permission, «حتّى یُؤذن لكم», just as returning is necessary immediately upon the appearance of signs of displeasure. «اذا قیل لكم ارجعوا فارجعوا»

3- Entering people's homes requires authorization and permission; whether the granter of permission is the homeowner and owner, or another authorized authority, such as the Islamic ruler. (The phrase «یؤذن» is in the passive voice to establish the principle of authorization, and the name of the granter of permission is not mentioned.)

4- Lingering behind people's doors is forbidden. «اِرجعُوا»

5- Not accepting an uninvited guest is permissible. «ارجِعوا»

6- We must accept the homeowner's excuse and not impose ourselves upon the homeowner. «اِرجِعُوا فارجِعوا»

7- Sincerity, intimacy, and having no undue expectation are among the blessings of Islamic ethics. «اِرجِعوا فارجِعوا»

8- Sometimes a person's good lies in facing rejection (being turned away). «اِرجعوا - هو اَزكى لكم» (Not every return is a defeat.)

9- The criterion in social relations is purity and cleanliness (Taharah). «اَزكى لكم» (The commands and prohibitions of Allah are the path to achieving purity.)

10- Allah's knowledge of our actions is a factor for both encouraging and threatening us. «بما تعملون علیم»

Ayah

لَّيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ أَن تَدْخُلُوا۟ بُيُوتًا غَيْرَ مَسْكُونَةٍ فِيهَا مَتَـٰعٌ لَّكُمْ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا تُبْدُونَ وَمَا تَكْتُمُونَ (۲۹)
There will be no sin upon you in entering [without announcing] uninhabited houses wherein you have goods belonging to you. Allah knows whatever you disclose and whatever you conceal. (29)

Key Points

The ruling regarding public places that do not have specific residents—such as mosques, hotels, caravanserais, shops, and offices, whose doors are open to everyone and which people benefit from frequenting—is separate from the ruling regarding private homes, and seeking permission is not necessary for entering those centers.(49)

49) Tafsir Burhan.

Messages

1- Going to public centers without a beneficial purpose, for loitering and wandering, is forbidden. «تدخلوا بیوتاً غیر مسكونة فیها متاع لكم»

2- When entering public centers, we must keep Allah in mind and know that Allah monitors our thoughts and actions. «واللّه یعلم ما تبدون و ما تكتمون»

Ayah

قُل لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يَغُضُّوا۟ مِنْ أَبْصَـٰرِهِمْ وَيَحْفَظُوا۟ فُرُوجَهُمْ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ أَزْكَىٰ لَهُمْ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ خَبِيرٌۢ بِمَا يَصْنَعُونَ (۳٠)
Tell the faithful men to cast down their looks and to guard their private parts. That is more decent for them. Allah is indeed well aware of what they do. (30)

Key Points

The word yaghuḍḍū (یغضّوا) is derived from the root ghaḍḍ (غضّ), meaning to diminish or lower. Just as Luqman said to his son: "و اغضض من صوتك" (And lower your voice), meaning lower your voice and do not speak loudly. Here too, the intent of ghaḍḍ al-baṣar (غضّ بصر) is not closing the eyes, but lowering the gaze, so that one does not look at a non-mahram (unrelated person).

The intent of furūj (فروج) is the private parts, which must be covered from the sight of others. Of course, the intent of preserving the furūj in other verses of the Quran is preservation from fornication (zina), but in this verse, based on narrations, it means preservation from the gaze.

Narrations concerning the gaze:

  • Imam Ali عليه السلام said: "لكم اوّل نَظَرة الى المرئة فلا تتّبعوها بنَظرة اُخرى واحذروا الفتنة" (The first glance at a woman is permissible for you, but do not follow it with another glance, and beware of temptation) (50). The first glance is permissible, but continuing the gaze is problematic, as it causes temptation (fitnah).
  • In a hadith, we read: All eyes will be weeping on the Day of Judgment except for three eyes: an eye that weeps out of fear of Allah, an eye that is closed off from sin, and an eye that remains vigilant in the way of Allah (51).
  • We read in Ṣaḥīḥ al-Bukhārī that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: Do not sit in the path of people. Some said: We are compelled to sit. He said: Then fulfill its right. They asked what its right was. He said: "غَضّ البَصر و كَفّ الاَذى و رَدّ السلام والامر بالمعروف و النهى عن المنكر" (Lowering the gaze, refraining from harm, returning the greeting, enjoining the good, and forbidding the evil). (This means) abandoning leering, refraining from harming people, responding to the greetings of others, enjoining the good, and forbidding the evil.
  • Jesus عليه السلام said: Beware of looking at a non-mahram, for it plants the seed of lust in the heart, and this is sufficient for a person to fall into temptation (52).
  • Imam Ali عليه السلام said: "العَین رائِدُ القلب" (The eye is the scout of the heart). The eye drags the heart after it. "العَین مَصائد الشیطان" (The eye is the snares of Satan). And turning away the gaze is the best way to distance oneself from desires (53).
  • The Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "النَظَر سَهْم مسموم مِن سهام ابلیس فمَن تَركَها خَوفاً من اللّه اعطاه اللّه ایماناً یَجِد حلاوة فى قلبه" (The [forbidden] gaze is a poisoned arrow from the arrows of Iblis. Whoever abandons it out of fear of Allah, Allah grants him a faith whose sweetness he finds in his heart) (54). A forbidden gaze is a poisoned arrow from the arrows of Satan. Whoever lowers his gaze out of fear of Allah, Allah grants him a faith whose sweetness he feels internally.
  • The Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: Whoever fills his eye with the forbidden, his eye will be filled with fire on the Day of Judgment, unless he repents (55).

(50) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq. (51) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq. (52) Tafsir Rūḥ al-Bayān. (53) Ghurar al-Ḥikam. (54) Biḥār, Vol. 104, p. 38. (55) Biḥār, 76, p. 336.

Messages

  1. The prerequisite of faith is preserving the gaze from the forbidden. "قل للمؤمنین یغضّوا..." (Tell the believing men to lower their gaze...)
  2. Instinctual attractions must be controlled. "یغضّوا من ابصارهم" (They should lower their gaze).
  3. We must prevent sin at its source and begin piety with the eye. "من ابصارهم" (from their eyes/gaze).
  4. A pure eye is the prelude to chastity. "یَغضّوا من ابصارهم و یحفظوا فروجهم" (They should lower their gaze and guard their private parts).
  5. Chastity is also necessary for men. "یَحفظوا فُروجهم" (They should guard their private parts).
  6. Leering and immodesty prevent man's spiritual growth. "یغضّوا - یحفظوا - ازكى" (They lower their gaze - they guard their private parts - that is purer).
  7. We are always in the presence of Allah. Let us not commit sin in the presence of Allah. "یغضّوا... اِنّ اللّه خبیر بما یصنعون" (They lower their gaze... Indeed, Allah is Acquainted with what they do).

Ayah

وَقُل لِّلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ يَغْضُضْنَ مِنْ أَبْصَـٰرِهِنَّ وَيَحْفَظْنَ فُرُوجَهُنَّ وَلَا يُبْدِينَ زِينَتَهُنَّ إِلَّا مَا ظَهَرَ مِنْهَا ۖ وَلْيَضْرِبْنَ بِخُمُرِهِنَّ عَلَىٰ جُيُوبِهِنَّ ۖ وَلَا يُبْدِينَ زِينَتَهُنَّ إِلَّا لِبُعُولَتِهِنَّ أَوْ ءَابَآئِهِنَّ أَوْ ءَابَآءِ بُعُولَتِهِنَّ أَوْ أَبْنَآئِهِنَّ أَوْ أَبْنَآءِ بُعُولَتِهِنَّ أَوْ إِخْوَٰنِهِنَّ أَوْ بَنِىٓ إِخْوَٰنِهِنَّ أَوْ بَنِىٓ أَخَوَٰتِهِنَّ أَوْ نِسَآئِهِنَّ أَوْ مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُهُنَّ أَوِ ٱلتَّـٰبِعِينَ غَيْرِ أُو۟لِى ٱلْإِرْبَةِ مِنَ ٱلرِّجَالِ أَوِ ٱلطِّفْلِ ٱلَّذِينَ لَمْ يَظْهَرُوا۟ عَلَىٰ عَوْرَٰتِ ٱلنِّسَآءِ ۖ وَلَا يَضْرِبْنَ بِأَرْجُلِهِنَّ لِيُعْلَمَ مَا يُخْفِينَ مِن زِينَتِهِنَّ ۚ وَتُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ جَمِيعًا أَيُّهَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ (۳۱)
And tell the faithful women to cast down their looks and to guard their private parts, and not to display their charms, beyond what is [acceptably] visible, and let them draw their scarfs over their bosoms, and not display their charms except to their husbands, or their fathers, or their husband’s fathers, or their sons, or their husband’s sons, or their brothers, or their brothers’ sons, or their sisters’ sons, or their women, or their slave girls, or male dependants lacking [sexual] desire, or children who are not yet conscious of female sexuality. And let them not thump their feet to make known their hidden ornaments. Rally to Allah in repentance, O faithful, so that you may be felicitous. (31)

Key Points

Although Allah has not referred to the details of the rulings of prayer (Salat) and charity (Zakat) in the Quran, entrusting their explanation and many other issues to the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, He has addressed the minutest details concerning family and educational matters.

Just as men must refrain from lustful glances toward women, women must also not look at non-mahram men with lust, but rather must control their gaze and cover their adornment and beauty from non-mahram men, except for the face and hands, which are naturally visible, and covering them would cause difficulties in performing daily life activities.

Khumur (خُمُر) is the plural of khimar (خِمار), meaning headscarf, and juyub (جُیوب) is the plural of jayb (جِیب), meaning neck and chest.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of the verse "یَغضُضنَ من ابصارهنّ و یحفظوا فروجهنّ" is that women should not look at the private parts of other women, and they should protect their own private parts from the gaze of others. (56)

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, in interpreting the phrase "الاّ لِبُعولتهنّ," said: Allah has cursed those women who do not adorn their face and hair for their husbands and do not fulfill their husbands' needs. (57)

The word irbah (اِربة) means need or necessity. The intent of uli al-irbah (اولى الاربة) is servants and attendants who, due to advanced age or physical and psychological conditions, have no desire for a spouse. (58)

Repentance (Tawbah) is sometimes related to an individual sin and sometimes related to changing a widespread corrupt culture. The verse apparently concerns the second type, because during the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), women's necks and chests were uncovered. (59)

Maintaining the hijab and abandoning ostentation yields many benefits, some of which include: spiritual tranquility, strengthening family bonds, preserving lineage, preventing assault and transgression, preventing sexually transmitted and psychological diseases, lowering the statistics of divorce, suicide, illegitimate children, and abortion, eliminating negative rivalries, preserving the personality and humanity of women, and salvation from the lustful eyes and hearts that have engulfed the West and the East today.

56) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 57) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 58) Kafi, Vol. 5, p. 523. 59) Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr Razi.

Messages

1- There is no difference between men and women regarding the necessity of modesty, chastity, and abandoning unlawful gazing. "یَغضّوا - یَغضُضن - یَحفظوا - یَحفظن"

2- The display of women's adornment in society is forbidden. "ولا یُبدین زینتهنّ"

3- The visibility of parts of the body that are naturally exposed (face, hands, or feet) is permissible. "الاّ ما ظَهَر منها"

4- In this verse, among the types of coverings, the head covering and headscarf are mentioned, which indicates the importance of covering the head, neck, and chest. "و لیضربن بخُمُرهنّ على جیوبهنّ"

5- When legislating, necessities, realities, and needs must be considered. (If covering the face and hands up to the wrist were obligatory, it would be difficult for women in general.) "الاّ ما ظَهر منها"

6- Islam gives a positive response to the instinctive needs of humans. "الاّ لبُعولتهنّ"

7- Wearing any kind of shoes or clothing that, when walking, causes a woman's jewelry and beauty to become apparent is not permissible. "ولا یضربن بارجلهنّ لیعلم ما یخفین من زینتهنّ"

8- A woman has the right of ownership. "مَلَكت ایمانهنّ"

9- A woman may show her adornment to Muslim women, but she must cover herself in the presence of disbelieving women. "نسائهنّ" (Because they might describe what they saw of the Muslim woman to their husbands or strange men.)

10- The hijab and covering of a woman is due to the sexual inclinations of men. Therefore, covering is not necessary in front of men who have no desire for a spouse. "غیر اولى الاِربَة من الرجال"

11- Hijab and covering are obligatory. "ولیَضربنَ بخُمُرهنّ"

12- Any action that reveals a woman's adornment or makes others aware of it is forbidden. "لیعلم مایُخفین من زینتهنّ"

13- While maintaining hijab, the presence of women in society is not prohibited. "ولا یُبدینَ زینتهنّ" Just as the participation of women in congregational prayer is permissible. "و اركَعى مع الرّاكعین" (60)

14- The requirement of faith is repentance to Allah. "توبوا الى اللّه جمیعاً ایّها المؤمنون"

15- The path to salvation is repentance and returning from deviant paths. "تُوبوا... تفلحون"

60) Al Imran, 43.

Ayah

وَأَنكِحُوا۟ ٱلْأَيَـٰمَىٰ مِنكُمْ وَٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ مِنْ عِبَادِكُمْ وَإِمَآئِكُمْ ۚ إِن يَكُونُوا۟ فُقَرَآءَ يُغْنِهِمُ ٱللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ وَٰسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۳۲)
Marry off those who are single among you, and the upright among your male and female slaves. If they are poor, Allah will enrich them out of His grace, and Allah is all-bounteous, all-knowing. (32)

Key Points

“Ayama” (اَیامى) is the plural of “Ayyim” (أیّم), pronounced like “Qayyim” (قَیّم), meaning someone who does not have a spouse, whether male or female, virgin or widowed. The best mediation is intercession and mediation in the matter of marriage. As stated in a narration: Whoever helps another person get married (as a groom or a bride) is in the shade of Allah’s Throne. (61) If parents who have the means do not arrange a marriage for their child, and the child commits a sin, the parents are partners in that sin. (62)

Marriage in Islam Islam considers marriage a sacred matter, contrary to Christianity, which values celibacy. Thus, the Pope and Christian clergy must not marry, and the permission for marriage among Christians is due to preventing greater corruption, or, as the saying goes, repelling the worse evil with the lesser evil.

Many narrations have been reported concerning marriage, a few of which we mention: * Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The expansion of livelihood is under the shade of marriage. * Whoever does not marry for fear of poverty has harbored ill thoughts about Allah. * Two units (rak'ahs) of prayer performed by someone who is married are better than seventy units of prayer performed by someone who is unmarried. (63)

Benefits of Marriage

  1. Affection and Tranquility The Quran states: «وَ مِنْ آیاتِهِ أَنْ خَلَقَ لَكُمْ مِنْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ أَزْواجاً لِتَسْكُنُوا إِلَیْها وَ جَعَلَ بَیْنَكُمْ مَوَدَّةً وَ رَحْمَةً» (64): And among His signs is that He created for you mates from yourselves, that you may find tranquility in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy.

One of the needs of humanity is affection and intimacy, and thus loneliness is difficult for a person. He needs someone who is trustworthy, sympathetic, kind, and a keeper of secrets, so that he can find intimacy with them and purely dedicate his love to them. The best person who can fulfill this need is the spouse, whether wife or husband. The Quran mentioned only this benefit among the many benefits of marriage, which indicates that it is the most important benefit.

  1. Preservation of Chastity and Immunity from Sin The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said in one narration: «مَنْ تَزَوَّجَ فقد أَحْرَزَ نِصْفَ دِینِهِ» (65) Whoever marries has preserved half of his religion. And in another narration, He said: «مَنْ أَحَبَّ أَنْ یَلْقَى اللَّهَ طَاهِراً مُطَهَّراً فَلْیَلْقَهُ بِزَوْجَةٍ» (66) Whoever loves to meet Allah on the Day of Judgment pure and purified, let him take a spouse.

  2. Physical and Mental Health Scientists say: The correct and timely satisfaction of the sexual instinct is a necessity for the health of the body and soul, and abandoning it causes psychological illnesses and diseases such as: nervous weakness, anxiety, depression, despair, tension, irritability, pessimism, isolation, and sometimes indigestion and headaches. (67)

  3. Continuation of the Lineage The continuation of the human lineage depends on marriage and conjugal life. In Islam, the matter of raising the next generation is considered one of the duties of parents, for which they will be questioned on the Day of Judgment.

  4. Independence Before marriage, the boy and girl are considered part of the family and subordinate to their parents, whereas after marriage, they make decisions independently and accept responsibility.

  5. Societal Security If the foundation of the family is strong, corruption, rebellion, murder, theft, crime, rape, and suicide are greatly reduced, because most of these incidents originate from individuals deprived of a warm family environment. (68)

Care in Choosing a Spouse Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: A woman is like a necklace that you place around your neck, so be careful.

Emotions should not dominate when choosing a spouse. Do not rush, and proceed after thought, consultation, and discussion. A large portion of family disputes stems from a lack of care in choosing a spouse. The spouse is a life partner, and their influence lasts until the end of life and until the Day of Judgment. Usually, superficial marriages that occur through a single encounter or acquaintance on a bus or in a park do not have a good outcome.

Characteristics of a Good Spouse

  1. Religion and Faith A. The Quran states: «و لا تَنْكِحُوا الْمُشْرِكاتِ حَتّى یُؤمِنَّ وَ لأَمَةٌ مُؤْمِنَةٌ خَیْرٌ مِن مُشْرِكَةٍ و لو أعْجَبَتْكُمْ و لا تُنْكِحُوا المُشرِكِینَ حَتّى یُؤمِنُوا وَ لَعَبْدٌ مُؤمِنٌ خَیْرٌ مِنْ مُشْرِكٍ و لو أعْجَبَكُمْ اولئِكَ یَدْعُونَ الَى النّارِ وَ اللّهُ یَدْعُوا إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ» (69) And do not marry polytheist women until they believe. And a believing slave woman is better than a polytheist woman, even though she might impress you (with her beauty). And do not give (your women) in marriage to polytheist men until they believe. And a believing slave is better than a polytheist man, even though he might impress you (with his wealth and beauty). Those invite to the Fire, but Allah invites to Paradise.

B. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «إِذَا تَزَوَّجَ الرَّجُلُ الْمَرْأَةَ لِجَمَالِهَا أَوْ مَالِهَا وُكِلَ إِلَى ذَلِكَ وَ إِذَا تَزَوَّجَهَا لِدِینِهَا رَزَقَهُ اللَّهُ الْجَمَالَ وَ الْمَالَ» (70); When a man marries a woman for her beauty or her wealth, he is left to that (alone). But when he marries her for her religion, Allah grants him both beauty and wealth as His provision.

  1. Good and Pleasant Morality Imam Ridha عليه السلام, in response to one of his companions who wrote that he intended to marry someone who was not well-mannered, said: If he/she has bad manners, do not marry him/her. (71)

Morality in life leads to the sweetness of life and the growth of the family. Qualities such as: truthfulness, trustworthiness, pleasant speech, benevolence, forgiveness and self-sacrifice, patience, conscientiousness, nobility, low expectations, gentleness, etc., are human merits that Islam has emphasized.

  1. Family Nobility/Origin The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: «أیُّها النَّاسُ ایَّاكُم وخَضْرَاءَ الدِّمَن. قیل یا رسولَ اللَّهِ و ما خَضْرَاءُ الدِّمَنِ؟ قالَ: المَرأةُ الحَسْنَاءُ فى مَنْبِتِ السَّوْءِ» (72); O people, beware of the greenery that grows on dung heaps. They asked: O Messenger of Allah, what is meant by that? He said: Beautiful women who have grown up in an impure family.

  2. Literacy and Education Knowledge and learning are a perfection for a human being and an advantage for a spouse. It is easier to reach understanding and plan a better future with an educated person. Of course, not every educated person is prudent. Unfortunately, schools and universities in our country do not teach life skills.

  3. Physical Health Physical health is one of Allah’s great blessings and an advantage in a spouse. It is stated in narrations: Do not marry those who have contagious diseases. Of course, marrying self-sacrificing veterans (those injured in war) requires merit, worthiness, and selflessness.

  4. Age Compatibility A person has different conditions at different ages. Sexual desires are also not the same at every age, so there should not be a significant difference between the age of the girl and the boy, as this creates problems in life.

Mahr (Dowry)

  1. The man offers the Mahr to the woman to prove his sincerity, which is why it is called Sadaq (proof of sincerity), and it is the woman's support throughout life and after divorce or the death of her husband.
  2. The amount of Mahr is not specified in Islam, but heavy Mahrs have been condemned in the Ahadith, and it is stated that excessive Mahr causes resentment and enmity. (73)
  3. The man must try to pay the woman's Mahr at the earliest opportunity when he becomes financially able.
  4. The woman is the owner of the Mahr and can do whatever she wishes with it, whether forgiving it or using it for trade.
  5. In narrations, a man who intends not to pay the Mahr has been referred to as a thief and an adulterer. (74)

Jahiziyah (Bridal Trousseau)

  1. The groom has no right, neither religiously nor legally, to demand the bridal trousseau (Jahiz), although it is customary for the bride's family to purchase the household goods and necessities.
  2. The trousseau is meant to provide comfort for the young couple, not for boasting or showing off.
  3. The list of the trousseau of Lady Fatimah: 1- One dress 2- One large headscarf 3- One black cloak (hullah) 4- Two mattresses 5- One bed made of palm fiber 6- Four pillows 7- One mat 8- One hand mill 9- One copper bowl 10- One leather waterskin 11- One washing basin 12- One milk bowl 13- One drinking vessel 14- One curtain 15- One ewer (Aftabeh) 16- One jug 17- One hide for a rug 18- Two earthenware jars 19- One cloak (Aba).

Marriage and Wedding Ceremony in Islam In Islam, it is recommended that the marriage contract (Aqd) and wedding ceremony be held in the presence of people and accompanied by joy. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله performed the marriage contract of Fatimah Zahra عليها السلام in the mosque, in the presence of the Ansar and the Muhajirin, and ordered dates to be distributed among those present.

During the wedding of Lady Zahra عليها السلام, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله told His wives: Adorn Fatimah and perfume her, and furnish her room so that we may send her to her husband's house. (75) He also told the women of the Muhajirin and Ansar: Accompany Fatimah to her husband's house and rejoice, but be careful not to say anything that Allah is not pleased with.
