Messages

1- The family and society are responsible for the marriage of unmarried individuals. «و اَنكِحوا»

2- Marriage in Islam is a sacred and emphasized matter. «و اَنكحوا»

3- It is not necessary for the proposal to come from a specific party; either side of the marriage can take the initiative. «و اَنكحوا»

4- Merely advising against forbidden glances is not enough; action must be taken for the marriage of young people. «یَغضّوا، یَغضُضن، انكحوا»

5- Arrange marriages for widowed women. «و اَنكِحوا الایامى» (“Ayama” includes them as well.)

6- Having competence is a condition for marriage. «والصّالحین» (Meaning both parties must be competent to manage a shared life.)

7- Societal action to marry off righteous individuals is an encouragement for reckless and dissolute youth to also place themselves within the circle of the righteous. «والصّالحین...» (Provided that competence refers to moral and ideological competence.)

8- In fulfilling sexual needs, there is no difference among human beings (woman, man, male slave, and female slave). «منكم، عبادكم، امائكم»

9- Poverty should not prevent action in the matter of marriage. «اِن یكونوا فقراء...»

10- In case of poverty, proceed with marriage and rely upon Allah. «یُغنِهم اللّه»

11- Poverty is not a defect for the bride and groom. «ان یكونوا فقراء...»

12- Allah has promised to provide for the livelihood of the bride and groom, and marriage is a means of expanding and blessing life. «یغنهم اللّه»

13- The fulfillment of Divine promises originates from the source of His endless bounty. «فضله - واسع»

14- The growth and expansion that Allah grants His servants is based on His knowledge and wisdom. «واسعٌ علیم»

Ayah

وَلْيَسْتَعْفِفِ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَجِدُونَ نِكَاحًا حَتَّىٰ يُغْنِيَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ ۗ وَٱلَّذِينَ يَبْتَغُونَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ مِمَّا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُكُمْ فَكَاتِبُوهُمْ إِنْ عَلِمْتُمْ فِيهِمْ خَيْرًا ۖ وَءَاتُوهُم مِّن مَّالِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِىٓ ءَاتَىٰكُمْ ۚ وَلَا تُكْرِهُوا۟ فَتَيَـٰتِكُمْ عَلَى ٱلْبِغَآءِ إِنْ أَرَدْنَ تَحَصُّنًا لِّتَبْتَغُوا۟ عَرَضَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۚ وَمَن يُكْرِههُّنَّ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ إِكْرَٰهِهِنَّ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۳۳)
Those who cannot afford marriage should be continent until Allah enriches them out of His grace. As for those who seek an emancipation deal from among your slaves, make such a deal with them if you know any good in them, and give them out of the wealth of Allah which He has given you. Do not compel your female slaves to prostitution when they desire to be chaste, seeking the transitory wares of the life of this world. Should anyone compel them, Allah will be forgiving and merciful to them following their compulsion. (33)

Key Points

Initially, one must recommend marriage to everyone who is able, and then recommend chastity and purity to the unmarried. (The Ayah «وانكحوا» came before the Ayah «ولیستعفف»). As mentioned in narrations: During the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), slave girls were forced into prostitution to earn income. Therefore, Allah says: Instead of forcing slave girls into sin, provide the means for their marriage. Regarding the treatment of war captives, we can adopt one of the following paths: A: Free all captives at once, in which case they would return to war. B: Kill them all, which is inhumane. C: Gather the captives in one area and cover their expenses from the Muslim treasury (Bayt al-Mal). (In this plan, the innocent must pay the costs of the criminals). D: Distribute them among Muslims so that they may observe the conduct of Islam and Muslims, gradually be influenced, and become Muslims, and then free them through various means. This plan is the best, and it is the plan of Islam. Furthermore, the system of war at that time was such that war captives were used as slaves, and Islam reciprocated in kind, just as today captives are placed in camps worldwide, and we must accept international laws of war as long as they do not conflict with our religion.

Messages

  1. Lack of access to a spouse is not a justification for sin; patience and chastity are necessary. «ولیَستَعفف»

  2. Wherever the sense of danger is greater, a specific recommendation is necessary. «ولیَستَعفِف الّذین لایَجِدونَ نكاحا» (Since unmarried individuals are more exposed to immorality and vice than others, Allah addresses them specifically in the command for chastity.)

  3. Young people should not resort to acquiring wealth through unlawful means for the sake of marriage possibilities; they must demonstrate chastity and piety. «حتّى یُغنیهم اللّه من فضله»

  4. In preserving public chastity and purity, young people must protect themselves, «و لیَستَعفف», the family and government must take action, «واَنكحوا الاَیامى», and the wealthy members of society must also rise up. «و آتوهم من مال اللّه»

  5. Have a written contract when dealing with your subordinates. «مما مَلَكت اَیمانكم فكاتِبوهم»

  6. Islam has various plans for the freedom of slaves. One of them is the contract for self-purchase (manumission contract). «فكاتبوهم»

  7. Do not consider possessions and wealth as your own; rather, they belong to Allah, Who has entrusted them to you for a few days. «مال اللّه»

  8. The wealthy have a responsibility regarding the marriage of their subordinates. «و آتوهم من مال اللّه الّذى آتاكم»

  9. Employing subordinates in unlawful acts is forbidden. «و لا تكرهوا فَتَیاتكم على البِغاء»

  10. Worldly ambition is the source of sins. «لا تكرهوا ... لتبتغوا عرض الحیاة الدّنیا»

  11. Accumulating wealth through illegitimate means is not permissible. «لتَبتغوا عرض الحیاة الدّنیا»

  12. The world is fleeting. «عرض الحیاة الدّنیا» (The word «عرض» means temporary and transient.)

  13. Values must not be sacrificed for the sake of wealth and materialism. «لاتكرهوا... لتبتغوا»

  14. The sanctity of slave girls is like that of free women. «لا تكرهوا فَتَیاتكم على البغاء»

  15. Purity and chastity are the highest values for a woman. «اِن أرَدنَ تحصّناً»

  16. The elders are the source of some sins. «لاتكرهوا... أردن تحصّناً»

  17. Individuals forced into sin are forgiven. «فاِنّ اللّه من بعد اكراههنّ غفورٌ رحیم»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ أَنزَلْنَآ إِلَيْكُمْ ءَايَـٰتٍ مُّبَيِّنَـٰتٍ وَمَثَلًا مِّنَ ٱلَّذِينَ خَلَوْا۟ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ وَمَوْعِظَةً لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ (۳٤)
Certainly We have sent down to you manifest signs and a description of those who passed before you, and an advice for the Godwary. (34)

Messages

1- Allah establishes the conclusive proof (Hujjah) with the people. «اَنزلنا الیكم آیات مبیّنات»

2- The verses of the Quran are light and illuminating. «آیات مبیّنات»

3- The history of those who passed away is a lamp for the path of those who come later. «مثلاً من الّذین خَلَوا من قبلكم»

4- The Quran is a comprehensive book. «آیات - مثلاً - موعظة»

5- Accepting admonition requires a ready heart. «موعظةً للمتّقین»

6- The pious (Muttaqin) are also in need of admonition. «موعظةً للمتّقین»

Ayah

۞ ٱللَّهُ نُورُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ مَثَلُ نُورِهِۦ كَمِشْكَوٰةٍ فِيهَا مِصْبَاحٌ ۖ ٱلْمِصْبَاحُ فِى زُجَاجَةٍ ۖ ٱلزُّجَاجَةُ كَأَنَّهَا كَوْكَبٌ دُرِّىٌّ يُوقَدُ مِن شَجَرَةٍ مُّبَـٰرَكَةٍ زَيْتُونَةٍ لَّا شَرْقِيَّةٍ وَلَا غَرْبِيَّةٍ يَكَادُ زَيْتُهَا يُضِىٓءُ وَلَوْ لَمْ تَمْسَسْهُ نَارٌ ۚ نُّورٌ عَلَىٰ نُورٍ ۗ يَهْدِى ٱللَّهُ لِنُورِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَيَضْرِبُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْأَمْثَـٰلَ لِلنَّاسِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌ (۳٥)
Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth. The parable of His Light is a niche wherein is a lamp—the lamp is in a glass, the glass as it were a glittering star—lit from a blessed olive tree, neither eastern nor western, whose oil almost lights up, though fire should not touch it. Light upon light. Allah guides to His Light whomever He wishes. Allah draws parables for mankind, and Allah has knowledge of all things. (35)

Key Points

Nūr (Light) means something that is both luminous in itself and causes other things to be luminous. In Islamic culture, certain matters have been introduced as Nūr: the Quran, knowledge, intellect, faith, guidance, Islam, the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, and the infallible Imams علیهم السلام.

Mishkāt means a niche (for a lamp). Durrī means bright and radiant. Zayt refers to olive oil, which was a means of illumination. If the olive tree is in the middle of a garden, receiving sunlight throughout the day, its oil becomes better. But if it is on the edges of the garden or farm, it receives sun either in the morning or the evening. (This explains) «زیتونةٍ لاشرقیّةٍ و لا غربیّة» (an olive tree neither of the East nor of the West).

«اللّه نور السموات و الارض» (Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth) means: Allah is the Guide of the heavens and the earth. This is because the guidance of creation is also mentioned in other verses: «أعطى كل شى ء خلقه ثمّ هَدى» (He gave every thing its creation, then guided [it]) (76).

And the phrase «نورٌ على نور» (Light upon Light) means continuous and successive guidance. And at the end of the verse, He also says: «یَهدى اللّه لنوره» (Allah guides to His Light whomever He wills).

In this verse, the Quran likens Allah to Light. A brief study reveals that light possesses the following properties and characteristics:

1- Light is the most subtle entity in the material world and the source of beauty and delicacy.

2- Light possesses the highest speed in the material world, traveling at three hundred thousand kilometers per second, capable of circling the Earth seven times in the blink of an eye (i.e., less than one second). For this reason, extraordinarily vast and staggering astronomical distances are measured only by the speed of light, and their unit of measurement is the light-year, meaning the distance light travels in one year.

3- Light is the means of clarifying objects and observing beings, and without it, nothing can be seen. Therefore, it is both zāhir (manifest) and muẓhir (that which manifests others).

4- Sunlight, which is the most important light in our world, nourishes flowers and plants, and is indeed the secret to the survival of all living beings.

5- All energies present in the environment (except atomic energy) originate from sunlight. The movement of winds, the falling of rain, the flow of rivers and waterfalls, and ultimately, the movement of all living beings, upon closer inspection, trace back to sunlight. The source of warmth and heat, and that which keeps the habitat of beings warm, is sunlight itself. Even the heat of fire derived from wood, coal, or oil and its derivatives, comes from the warmth of the sun.

6- Sunlight destroys various types of microbes and harmful creatures. If the radiation of this blessed light did not exist, the Earth would turn into a massive hospital where all its inhabitants would be grappling with death.

In short, the more we look at this strange phenomenon of the universe—light—and the more precise we become, the more apparent its invaluable effects and immense blessings become.

But it is strange that despite all this scientific progress, humanity remains incapable of knowing the reality of light, which is a material thing, yet seeks to know the Essence and Reality of God. Perhaps this is one of the reasons for likening God to Light.

Now, if we wish to choose an analogy or likeness from sensible beings for the pure Essence of Allah (although His magnificent station is far superior to any likeness or parallel), can we use any word other than Nūr (Light)? He is the God Who is the Creator of all existence, the Illuminator of the universe. All beings are alive through His blessing, and all creatures are sustained by His bounty, such that if He were to withdraw His grace from them for a moment, they would all sink into the darkness of annihilation and non-existence.

Interestingly, every being acquires luminosity to the extent that it is connected to Him: The Quran is Light, because it is His Word. Islam is Light, because it is His religion. The Prophets are Light, because they are His messengers. The Imams are Divine Lights, because they are the guardians of His religion after the Prophets. Faith is Light, because it is the secret to connection with Him. Knowledge is Light, because it is the means of becoming acquainted with Him (77).

76) Taha, 50. 77) Tafsir Namūneh.

Messages

1- The universe is the grace of Allah and a ray of His Light; its stability and survival are from Him. «اللّه نور السموات...»

2- The Light of Allah is both expansive and stable. (The light inside the glass is both expansive and protected from being extinguished.) «المصباح فى زُجاجة»

3- The olive is a blessed tree. «شَجرةٍ مُباركةٍ زَیتونة»

4- The Light of Allah originates from His Essence, not from external factors. «یَكاد زَیتُها یضى ء و لو لم تمسسهُ نار»

5- Although Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth, and the source of creation and guidance for all existence, His place on earth is the pure and transparent hearts of the believers, which are illuminated by the light of faith. The greater the sincerity of the faith, the greater the radiance. «یهدى اللّه لنوره من یشاء...» (Allah guides to His Light whomever He wills...).

Ayah

فِى بُيُوتٍ أَذِنَ ٱللَّهُ أَن تُرْفَعَ وَيُذْكَرَ فِيهَا ٱسْمُهُۥ يُسَبِّحُ لَهُۥ فِيهَا بِٱلْغُدُوِّ وَٱلْـَٔاصَالِ (۳٦)
In houses Allah has allowed to be raised and wherein His Name is celebrated; He is glorified therein, morning and evening, (36)

Key Points

In Ziyarat Jami'ah, addressing the infallible Imams (علیهم السلام), we read: "He placed you in houses which Allah has permitted to be raised..." Therefore, the shrines (Harams) of the infallible Imams (علیهم السلام) are one of the manifestations of this Ayah. The word turfa'u (تُرفع) in the Ayah can also mean physical elevation, similar to the Ayah: «اذ یَرفع ابراهیم القواعِدَ من البیت و اسماعیل»(۷۸) where Prophet Abraham raised the foundations of the Ka'bah. Imam Baqir (علیه السلام) said: The meaning of buyut (houses) in this Ayah refers to the houses of the Prophets, the wise men, and the infallible Imams. (۷۹) And Tha'labi narrates in his Tafsir: One day the Noble Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله) was reciting this Ayah. Abu Bakr stood up and asked: Is the house of Fatimah (علیها السلام) and Ali (علیه السلام) among the manifestations of this Ayah? He replied: Yes, it is among the best manifestations of this Ayah. (۸۰) Imam Kadhim (علیه السلام) said: The meaning of bil-ghuduwwi wal-asal (بالغدوّ و الآصال - in the mornings and the evenings) in the Ayah refers to the times of prayer. (۸۱) In the culture of the Quran, the word tasbih (glorification) has been used in place of prayer in several instances, such as: «وسبّح بالعشىّ والابكار»(۸۲), and «و سَبّح بحَمدِ ربّك قبلَ طلوع الشمس و قبل الغروب»(۸۳)

78) Al-Baqarah, 127. 79) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 80) Tafsir Ruh al-Ma'ani. 81) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 23, p. 326. 82) Al 'Imran, 41. 83) Qaf, 39.

Messages

1- Access to the light of Allah's guidance is achieved through frequenting mosques. «اللّه نور... یهدى اللّه لنوره... فى بیوت...»

2- Places are not equal, and some have superiority over others. «فى بیوت أذن اللّه أن تُرفَع»

3- Mosques must be better and superior to other houses. «أن تُرفَع»

4- Greatness and majesty belong exclusively to Allah and to those matters He permits. «أذن اللّه أن تُرفَع»

5- The primary purpose for using mosques is prayer and the remembrance of Allah, not other ceremonies. «یُذكرُ فیها اسمه»

6- The doors of the mosque must be open during prayer times, and no one has the right to close them. «یسبّح له فیها بالغُدوّ و الآصال»

7- The glorification of Allah must be repeated every morning and evening. «یسبّح... بالغُدوّ و الآصال» (The element of time and place is effective in worship.)

Ayah

رِجَالٌ لَّا تُلْهِيهِمْ تِجَـٰرَةٌ وَلَا بَيْعٌ عَن ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ وَإِقَامِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَإِيتَآءِ ٱلزَّكَوٰةِ ۙ يَخَافُونَ يَوْمًا تَتَقَلَّبُ فِيهِ ٱلْقُلُوبُ وَٱلْأَبْصَـٰرُ (۳۷)
by men whom neither trade nor bargaining distracts from the remembrance of Allah and the maintenance of prayer and the giving of zakat. They are fearful of a day wherein the hearts and the sights will be transformed, (37)

Key Points

The word rijāl (رِجال) in this Ayah includes all servants of Allah, whether male or female. Tijārah (تجارت) is continuous trade for the purpose of gaining profit, while bay‘ (بیع) is periodic buying and selling to meet life's necessities. (84) We read in a Hadith: The intent behind those mentioned in this Ayah are merchants who abandon trade upon hearing the call to prayer (Adhan) and proceed toward the prayer (Salat). (85) Trade is not the only factor that preoccupies a person and distracts him from the remembrance of Allah; other factors are also mentioned in the Holy Quran, including: rivalry in increase (takāthur), vain desires, children, and bad company.

84) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Those whose trade does not distract them are great men. (Rijālun (رجالٌ) is used with tanwin, which is a sign of greatness and majesty.)

2- If faith and strong will exist, tools and means have no effect. «لا تُلهیهم تجارة»

3- The Islamic market must close during the time of prayer. «لا تُلهیهم تجارة»

4- Even permissible (halal) and lawful acts should not distract a person from the remembrance of Allah, let alone disliked (makruh) or forbidden (haram) acts. «لا تُلهیهم تجارة»

5- Abandoning trade is not a virtue; rather, trade accompanied by the remembrance of Allah, prayer, and Zakat is a virtue. «لا تُلهیهم تجارة... عن ذكر اللّه و...» (The men of Allah strive for worldly life but are not heedless of the Hereafter.)

6- Belief in the Hereafter minimizes the world in the eyes of a person. «لا تُلهیهم... یخافون یوماً»

7- Although prayer (Salat) is the remembrance of Allah, it is mentioned separately due to its importance. «ذكر اللّه و اقام الصلوة»

8- Prayer (Salat) and Zakat are side by side, and those who deserve praise are the ones who practice both. «اِقام الصّلوة و ایتاء الزّكاة»

9- The world should not distract you from the Hereafter. «یَخافون یوماً»

10- The scenes of the Day of Judgment make eyes humble and hearts restless (or overturned). «تَتقلّب فیه القلوب والابصار»

Ayah

لِيَجْزِيَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ أَحْسَنَ مَا عَمِلُوا۟ وَيَزِيدَهُم مِّن فَضْلِهِۦ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يَرْزُقُ مَن يَشَآءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ (۳۸)
so that Allah may reward them by the best of what they have done, and enhance them out of His grace, and Allah provides for whomever He wishes without any reckoning. (38)

Messages

1- Allah compensates for the deficiencies and shortcomings in the deeds of the men of God and grants them full reward. «لِیجزیَهم اللّه أحسن ما عملوا»

2- Grant a reward exceeding the deed. «و یزیدهم من فضله» (In Islam, it is recommended that you specify the wage, but when making the payment, give more than that amount.)

3- If you forgo worldly profit, you will attain the immeasurable profit of the Hereafter. «لاتُلهیهم تجارة - واللّه یرزق ... بغیرحساب» (The intent of "provision without reckoning" (رزقِ بى حساب) is an abundant reward that exceeds expectation.)

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَعْمَـٰلُهُمْ كَسَرَابٍۭ بِقِيعَةٍ يَحْسَبُهُ ٱلظَّمْـَٔانُ مَآءً حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَهُۥ لَمْ يَجِدْهُ شَيْـًٔا وَوَجَدَ ٱللَّهَ عِندَهُۥ فَوَفَّىٰهُ حِسَابَهُۥ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ سَرِيعُ ٱلْحِسَابِ (۳۹)
As for the faithless, their works are like a mirage in a plain, which the thirsty man supposes to be water. When he comes to it, he finds it to be nothing; but there he finds Allah, who will pay him his full account, and Allah is swift at reckoning. (39)

Key Points

The word qīʿah (قِیعة) means a large, flat land, like a desert. Ẓamʾān (ظَمأن) refers to someone who is extremely thirsty. The verbal noun of the word waffā (وَفّى) means the complete and unreduced fulfillment of something.

Messages

1- The condition for the acceptance of deeds is having faith, and human happiness is also dependent on faith. «والّذین كفروا اعمالهم كسَراب» (Disbelief causes deeds to be nullified and rendered void.)

2- The deeds of the disbelievers are deceptive. «كسراب...» (Look at the motives, not the manifestations and the apparent efforts.)

3- In teaching and education, the use of examples and analogies is effective. «اعمالهم كسراب...»

4- The Day of Judgment is the day when realities become manifest. «لم یجده شیئا و وجد اللّه عنده»

5- The motive behind all movements is reaching goodness, but the difference lies in realism versus fantasy. «یحسبه الظمئان ماءً»

6- The disbelievers will have no capital (assets) on the Day of Judgment. «لم یجده شیئاً»

7- In addition to belief, human deeds are effective in determining their destiny. «لیَجزیَهم أحسن ما عملوا... اعمالهم كسراب...»

8- On the Day of Judgment, the settling of accounts for individuals is both complete and precise, and swift. «فوَفّاه حسابه... سریع الحساب» (Unlike worldly accounting, which is either not precise and complete, or involves delay.)

Ayah

أَوْ كَظُلُمَـٰتٍ فِى بَحْرٍ لُّجِّىٍّ يَغْشَىٰهُ مَوْجٌ مِّن فَوْقِهِۦ مَوْجٌ مِّن فَوْقِهِۦ سَحَابٌ ۚ ظُلُمَـٰتٌۢ بَعْضُهَا فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ إِذَآ أَخْرَجَ يَدَهُۥ لَمْ يَكَدْ يَرَىٰهَا ۗ وَمَن لَّمْ يَجْعَلِ ٱللَّهُ لَهُۥ نُورًا فَمَا لَهُۥ مِن نُّورٍ (٤٠)
Or like the manifold darkness in a deep sea, covered by billow upon billow, overcast by clouds; manifold [layers of] darkness, one on top of another: when he brings out his hand, he can hardly see it. One whom Allah has not granted any light has no light. (40)

Key Points

The ordinary disbeliever (kāfir) is close to Islam, and there is hope for his return, like a simple disagreement that exists between two people whose matter is close to reconciliation and peace. However, the disbelievers who have many efforts and wrongful deeds in the path of disbelief, their return is very difficult. A disbeliever who has insisted upon his belief, and following his disbelief, has belittled the truth, waged wars, spent budgets, gathered people around himself, and become known by this title—his return to the fold of Islam is very difficult and remote. This is because every effort of his adds a wave of darkness upon the previous waves, and his acts of disbelief strengthen his disbelief. Instead of opening the way for them, it leads them further astray, and it is a veil upon previous veils. In contrast to the Divine Light (Ayah 35), which was "نورٌ على نور" (Light upon Light), this Ayah speaks of darkness upon darkness: "ظلماتٌ بعضها فوقَ بعض" (Darknesses, some above others).

Messages

1- Both positive and negative role models must be introduced. (The positive model: "رجالٌ لاتُلهیهِم..." [Men whom neither trade nor sale diverts them...] and the negative model: "والّذین كفروا... او كظلمات..." [But those who disbelieved... or like darknesses...])

2- The deed of the believer is light (nūr), and the deed of the disbeliever is darkness (ẓulumāt). "اعمالهم... كظلمات" (Their deeds... are like darknesses).

3- The good deeds of the disbelievers are like a mirage (sarāb), and their bad deeds are like darknesses (ẓulumāt). "كسَراب... او كظلمات" (Like a mirage... or like darknesses).

4- Knowledge does not render man independent of the light of revelation. If there is no Divine Light, no other light can save man. "فما له من نور" (For him there is no light).

Ayahs

أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُسَبِّحُ لَهُۥ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱلطَّيْرُ صَـٰٓفَّـٰتٍ ۖ كُلٌّ قَدْ عَلِمَ صَلَاتَهُۥ وَتَسْبِيحَهُۥ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا يَفْعَلُونَ (٤۱)
Have you not regarded that Allah is glorified by everyone in the heavens and the earth, and the birds spreading their wings. Each knows his prayer and glorification, and Allah knows best what they do. (41)
وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (٤۲)
To Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and toward Allah is the destination. (42)

Key Points

The Quran has repeatedly and explicitly stated the issue of the glorification (tasbih), prostration (sujud), and prayer (salat) of creatures, saying: You do not understand their glorification. (86) From these verses, it is understood that knowledge and consciousness are not exclusive to human beings. Although some commentators have considered the glorification of the creatures of existence to be a cosmic glorification (tasbih takwini) and the language of creation's state (zaban-e hal), the apparent meaning of the verses contradicts this notion. Allah has indirectly warned humanity several times in this verse:

A: The creatures of the heavens and the earth and the birds are engaged in glorification. Why is man heedless?! B: The glorification of creatures is conscious. Why does man lack presence of heart (khushu') in prayer?! C: Birds are engaged in glorification and prayer while flying in the air, but some humans, while flying in the sky, live in a state of heedlessness and intoxication!

We read in narrations: When Imam Sadiq عليه السلام heard the sound of a sparrow, He said: This bird is engaged in supplication (du'a) and glorification (tasbih). (87)

85) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 86) Al-Isra, 44. 87) Tafsir Kabir Fakhr Razi, Ruh al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Do not think only of yourselves; look around you and study and reflect upon existence. «ألم تَرَ»

2- Among the creatures of existence, birds, especially while flying, have a special attention toward Allah. «و الطّیر صافّات»

3- All creatures possess consciousness, and their prayer and glorification are conscious acts. «كلّ قد عَلِم صلاته»

4- Prayer is valuable only if the worshipper knows what he is saying and what he is doing. «كلّ قد علم صلاته»

5- The prayer and glorification of every creature are in a specific manner. «صلاته و تَسبیحه»

6- Allah is aware of the details of all actions. «علیم بما یفعلون»

7- The system of existence has both a Sovereign Ruler; «ولِلّه مُلك...» and a purpose. «و الى اللّه المصیر»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُزْجِى سَحَابًا ثُمَّ يُؤَلِّفُ بَيْنَهُۥ ثُمَّ يَجْعَلُهُۥ رُكَامًا فَتَرَى ٱلْوَدْقَ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ خِلَـٰلِهِۦ وَيُنَزِّلُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مِن جِبَالٍ فِيهَا مِنۢ بَرَدٍ فَيُصِيبُ بِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ وَيَصْرِفُهُۥ عَن مَّن يَشَآءُ ۖ يَكَادُ سَنَا بَرْقِهِۦ يَذْهَبُ بِٱلْأَبْصَـٰرِ (٤۳)
Have you not regarded that Allah drives the clouds, then He composes them, then He piles them up, whereat you see the rain issuing from its midst? And He sends down hail from the sky, out of the mountains that are in it, and He strikes with it whomever He wishes, and turns it away from whomever He wishes. The brilliance of its lightening almost takes away the sight. (43)

Key Points

«یُزجى» means slow movement accompanied by pushing (or driving). The word «رُكام» means accumulation, and «وَدق» means rain. The word «سَنا» means brilliance (or radiance), and "sanā barq" (سَنا برق) means the brilliance of lightning. If someone flies above the clouds by airplane, they will see that the masses of clouds are like mountains. This is one of the miracles of the Quran, which stated 14 centuries ago: «یُنزّل من السماء من جبال فیها من بَرَد» (He sends down from the sky, from mountains [of clouds] in it, hail). [Meaning:] From the mountain of cloud, We send down hail.

Messages

1- Reflection upon creation is the best way to know Allah and the source of love for Him. «ألم تَرَ...»

2- All movements in the natural world occur by divine power and will, and for a wise purpose. «یُزجى, یؤلّف, یَجعله»

3- Natural factors are the means for the realization of Allah's will, not substitutes for Allah. «یُزجى, یؤلّف, یَجعله, ینزّل»

4- The falling of rain and hail, and whether it is beneficial or harmful, is by the will of Allah. «فیُصیب به مَن یشاء و یَصرفه عن مَن یشاء»

Ayah

يُقَلِّبُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّيْلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَعِبْرَةً لِّأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَبْصَـٰرِ (٤٤)
Allah alternates the night and the day. There is indeed a lesson in that for those who have insight. (44)

Messages

1- The changes and alternation of night and day are not accidental; rather, they are based on a precise system. «یقلّب اللّه»

2- The phenomena of existence are sources of lesson; reflect upon them. «لَعِبرة لاُولى الابصار»

3- Whoever possesses insight can learn lessons from all times and places. «اللیل والنهار ... لاُولى الابصار»

4- Encouragement towards contemplation and the prohibition of superficiality are among the continuous recommendations of the Quran. «لاولى الابصار»

Ayah

وَٱللَّهُ خَلَقَ كُلَّ دَآبَّةٍ مِّن مَّآءٍ ۖ فَمِنْهُم مَّن يَمْشِى عَلَىٰ بَطْنِهِۦ وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَمْشِى عَلَىٰ رِجْلَيْنِ وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَمْشِى عَلَىٰٓ أَرْبَعٍ ۚ يَخْلُقُ ٱللَّهُ مَا يَشَآءُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (٤٥)
Allah created every animal from water. Among them are some that creep upon their bellies, and among them are some that walk on two feet, and among them are some that walk on four. Allah creates whatever He wishes. Indeed Allah has power over all things. (45)

Key Points

In this Ayah, Allah refers to the categories of animals: those that crawl, those that walk, and those that fly. Reflection upon the physical structure of animals is a step towards knowing Allah (God-knowledge). Imam Ali عليه السلام delivered instructive speeches in Nahj al-Balaghah regarding some animals, including the peacock and the ant. This Ayah follows the previous Ayah; meaning, just as the alternation of night and day is a lesson for people of insight, the creation of all animals from water is also a means of gaining a lesson. It is possible that the tanwin (nunation) in the word "ماء" (water) is, in technical terms, the tanwin of "diversification" (tanwin tanwi'), meaning that Allah created various types of creatures from various types of water and suitable liquids.(88)

88) Tafsir Rahnamā.

Messages

1- All moving creatures have been created by His will. «و اللّه خَلَقَ كلّ دابّة»

2- If man is not engaged in spiritual movement and growth, his outward movement will be just like that of other moving creatures. «یمشى، یمشى، یمشى»

3- The primary substance of all moving creatures is water, «ماء», but the manifestation of Allah's power is such that He creates so many diverse creatures from this simple substance. «و اللّه خلق كلّ دابّة من ماء فمنهم... و منهم... و منهم...»

Ayah

لَّقَدْ أَنزَلْنَآ ءَايَـٰتٍ مُّبَيِّنَـٰتٍ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَهْدِى مَن يَشَآءُ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (٤٦)
Certainly We have sent down illuminating signs, and Allah guides whomever He wishes to a straight path. (46)

Key Points

Regarding the difference between the words sabil (سَبیل) and tariq (طریق) compared to sirat (صراط), it has been said that sirat refers to a path whose traversing is smooth and which contains no complexity or difficulty. (89)

89) Furūq al-Lughah.

Messages

1- Allah has completed the proof (or established the conclusive argument) by sending down illuminating verses. «انزلنا...»

2- Divine verses are a means of guidance, but the essence of guidance is His work. «واللّه یهدى»

Ayah

وَيَقُولُونَ ءَامَنَّا بِٱللَّهِ وَبِٱلرَّسُولِ وَأَطَعْنَا ثُمَّ يَتَوَلَّىٰ فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ ۚ وَمَآ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ بِٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (٤۷)
They say, ‘We have faith in Allah and His Apostle, and we obey.’ Then, after that, a part of them refuse to comply, and they do not have faith. (47)

Key Points

This Ayah and the subsequent Ayahs concern the hypocrites of Medina who claimed to support the Prophet but were practically opposed (to him).

Messages

1- We should not rely on beautiful and deceptive slogans; faith and steadfastness are necessary. «یقولون... یَتولىّ»

2- The hypocrites both repeated their claim of faith and stated it emphatically. (The word «یقولون» is a present tense verb (فعل مضارع) and indicates repetition, and the repetition of the letter (باء) in «باللّه و بالرّسول» indicates emphasis.)

3- One who breaks a covenant has no religion. «یقولون - یَتولّى - ما اولئك بالمؤمنین»

4- Faith is not separate from action. «یَتولّى ... و ما اولئك بالمؤمنین» (Even the hypocrites used the word «أطَعنا» alongside the word «آمنّا», but in practice, they were indifferent.)

Ayahs

وَإِذَا دُعُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ إِذَا فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُم مُّعْرِضُونَ (٤۸)
When they are summoned to Allah and His Apostle that He may judge between them, behold, a part of them turn aside. (48)
وَإِن يَكُن لَّهُمُ ٱلْحَقُّ يَأْتُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ مُذْعِنِينَ (٤۹)
But if justice be on their side, they come compliantly to him. (49)

Key Points

The judgment and arbitration of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله is based on the command of Allah. As we read in Ayah 105 of Surah An-Nisa': «لتَحكمَ بین النّاس بما أراك اللّه» (that you may judge between the people by what Allah has shown you).

Messages

1- The sign of true faith is accepting the Prophet's ruling, and the sign of hypocrisy is turning away from it. «اذا دُعوا... فریق منهم مُعرضون»

2- Arbitration and judgment are among the functions of the Prophets. «لِیحكُمَ بینهم»

3- The ruling of the Prophet is the same as the ruling of Allah. (The verb «لیحكم» is used in the singular, not the dual form.)

4- Be fair in criticism and do not condemn everyone. «فَریق منهم»

5- Just judgment is not pleasing to a group of people. «فَریق منهم مُعرضون»

6- Do not rely on every declaration of adherence or interest. «واِن یكن لهم الحقّ یأتوا الیه...» (They come to the Prophet, but only if the Prophet's word is in their favor!)

7- The hypocrite is an opportunist and follows the prevailing trend. «اِن یكن لهم... مذعنین»

8- The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله would rule in favor of the hypocrites (where the truth was on their side). «لیحكم بینهم... ان یكن لهم الحقّ»

Ayah

أَفِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ أَمِ ٱرْتَابُوٓا۟ أَمْ يَخَافُونَ أَن يَحِيفَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَرَسُولُهُۥ ۚ بَلْ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ (٥٠)
Is there a sickness in their hearts? Do they have doubts, or fear that Allah and His Apostle will be unjust to them? Indeed, it is they who are the wrongdoers. (50)

Key Points

The word hayf (حیف) means deviation from moderation, stemming from oppression and injustice.

Messages

1- Lack of faith is a spiritual illness. «و ما اولئك بالمؤمنین... أفى قلوبهم مرض»

2- The reason for turning away from the judgment of the Prophet is threefold: spiritual illnesses, doubt, and suspicion. «أفى قلوبهم مرَضٌ أم ارتابوا أم یخافون أن یَحیف اللّه علیهم»

3- Doubt and suspicion towards Allah constitute oppression (injustice to oneself and the Prophets). «اولئك هم الظالمون»

Ayah

إِنَّمَا كَانَ قَوْلَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذَا دُعُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ أَن يَقُولُوا۟ سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا ۚ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ (٥۱)
All the response of the faithful, when they are summoned to Allah and His Apostle that He may judge between them, is to say, ‘We hear and obey.’ It is they who are the felicitous. (51)

Key Points

In the four preceding verses, [Allah] depicted the reaction of the hypocrites towards the judgment of the Prophet of Islam, and in this verse, He presents the reaction of the true believers, so that people may look within themselves to see which group they belong to when facing the decree of Allah.

Messages

1- The word and the path of the believer are one. «انّما»

2- Submission to the truth is the permanent characteristic of the believers. «كان قول المؤمنین... أن یقولوا...»

3- The believer is not concerned with the caller, but rather his attention is focused on the path to which he is being called. «دُعوا الى اللّه و رسوله»

4- What is important for the believer is acting upon the divine duty and accepting the judgment of Allah and the Messenger (whether it is to his benefit or his detriment). «لیحكُم بینهم»

5- Judgment (arbitration) is among the prerogatives of the Prophets. «لیَحكُم بینهم»

6- What is important is hearing accompanied by action, not merely hearing. «سَمِعنا و اَطعنا»

7- The obedience of true believers to the Prophet is based on awareness (indicated by the precedence of «سمعنا» over «اطعنا»).

8- Attaining salvation is achieved through submission to the command of Allah and obedience to Him. «سَمِعنا و اَطعنا و اولئك هم المفلحون»

9- Piety becomes evident during disputes and through the acceptance or rejection of rightful judgments. «اولئك هم المفلحون»

Ayah

وَمَن يُطِعِ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ وَيَخْشَ ٱللَّهَ وَيَتَّقْهِ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْفَآئِزُونَ (٥۲)
Whoever obeys Allah and His Apostle, and fears Allah and is wary of Him—it is they who will be the triumphant. (52)

Messages

1- Felicity is not dependent on race, age, gender, or tribe; whoever submits to Allah will attain salvation. «مَن یُطع... هم الفائزون»

2- The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is infallible, because unconditional obedience to him has been commanded. «مَن یُطع اللّه و رسوله»

3- What holds value is inner awe (Khushū') and outward piety (Taqwa). «یخش اللّه و یَتّقْه»

4- Obedience must be both to Allah and to the Messenger, and alongside that, there must be a spirit of awe (Khushū') and piety (Taqwa), not arrogance. «یُطع اللّه و رسوله و یَخش اللّه»

5- Those who fear God, practice piety, and are obedient to Allah and the Messenger, will certainly attain salvation. «فاولئك هم الفائزون»

Ayah

۞ وَأَقْسَمُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمَـٰنِهِمْ لَئِنْ أَمَرْتَهُمْ لَيَخْرُجُنَّ ۖ قُل لَّا تُقْسِمُوا۟ ۖ طَاعَةٌ مَّعْرُوفَةٌ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ خَبِيرٌۢ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ (٥۳)
They swear by Allah with solemn oaths that if you order them they will surely march out. Say, ‘Do not swear! Honourable obedience [is all that is expected of you]. Allah is indeed well aware of what you do.’ (53)

Messages

1- Do not trust every oath, because the hypocrites misuse sacred things. «واقسموا باللّه...»

2- The emptier a person's inside is, the greater his pretense is. «جَهْد ایمانهم»

3- Being a Muslim is not only about speech; action is also necessary. «طاعةٌ معروفة»

4- Prevent their misuse by refusing to accept the oaths of the hypocrites. «قل لاتقسموا»

5- Allah threatens the hypocrites that He is aware of your words and deeds. «ان اللّه خبیر بما تعلمون»

Ayah

قُلْ أَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱلرَّسُولَ ۖ فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ فَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْهِ مَا حُمِّلَ وَعَلَيْكُم مَّا حُمِّلْتُمْ ۖ وَإِن تُطِيعُوهُ تَهْتَدُوا۟ ۚ وَمَا عَلَى ٱلرَّسُولِ إِلَّا ٱلْبَلَـٰغُ ٱلْمُبِينُ (٥٤)
Say, ‘Obey Allah, and obey the Apostle.’ But if you turn your backs, [you should know that] he is only responsible for his burden and you are responsible for your own burden, and if you obey him, you will be guided, and the Apostle’s duty is only to communicate in clear terms. (54)

Key Points

The Prophet's commands are of two types: one, divine commands; "اطیعوا اللّه" (Obey Allah), and the other, governmental/executive commands; "اطیعوا الرّسول" (Obey the Messenger). In the Quran, we encounter instances where two commands are mentioned alongside each other, and both must be executed; acting upon only one is insufficient, including: * Faith and action. "آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات"(90) * Prayer and Zakat. "اقیموا الصلاة و آتوا الزّكاة"(91) * Gratitude to Allah and parents. "أن اشكر لى و لوالدیك"(92) * Obedience to Allah and the Messenger. "اطیعوا اللّه و اطیعوا الرّسول" which is mentioned in this Ayah. In the Quran, the phrase "و اِن تَولّوا" (But if they turn away) is used frequently, and in all instances, it reassures the Prophet not to be discouraged by the people's indifference toward His propagation (of the message).

90) Al-Baqarah, 25. 91) Al-Baqarah, 43. 92) Luqman, 14.

Messages

1- The Prophet is commanded to call the hypocrites to obedience. "قل اطیعوا..."

2- Obedience to the commands and prohibitions of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله is obligatory, just like obedience to the commands of Allah. "اطیعوا الرّسول"

3- The guidance of the Prophet is in harmony with the divine commands. "اطیعوا اللّه و اطیعوا الرّسول" Otherwise, Allah would not have commanded, "Obey Me and obey guidance that contradicts Me."

4- The people's adherence to or deviation from the commands of the Prophets does not affect the duty of those great figures. "فاِن تَولّوا فانّما علیه ما حمّل"

5- The responsibility of every individual relates to himself. "علیه ما حُمّل و علیكم ما حُمّلتم"

6- Guidance is achieved through obedience. "اِن تُطیعوه تهتدوا"

7- Prophets convey the message, they do not compel. "و ما على الرّسول الاّ البلاغ" Yes, the people's refusal to accept is not a reason for the messenger to stop conveying.

Ayah

وَعَدَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مِنكُمْ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ لَيَسْتَخْلِفَنَّهُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ كَمَا ٱسْتَخْلَفَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ وَلَيُمَكِّنَنَّ لَهُمْ دِينَهُمُ ٱلَّذِى ٱرْتَضَىٰ لَهُمْ وَلَيُبَدِّلَنَّهُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ خَوْفِهِمْ أَمْنًا ۚ يَعْبُدُونَنِى لَا يُشْرِكُونَ بِى شَيْـًٔا ۚ وَمَن كَفَرَ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْفَـٰسِقُونَ (٥٥)
Allah has promised those of you who have faith and do righteous deeds that He will surely make them successors in the earth, just as He made those who were before them successors, and He will surely establish for them their religion which He has approved for them, and that He will surely change their state to security after their fear, while they worship Me, not ascribing any partners to Me. Whoever is ungrateful after that—it is they who are the transgressors. (55)

Key Points

In many narrations from Imam Sajjad, Imam Baqir, and Imam Sadiq علیهم السلام, we read: The complete manifestation of this Ayah is the global government of Hadrat Mahdi عليه السلام (93), when Allah has promised that the people of faith will rule the world. In the Quran, the ultimate rule of the righteous has been explicitly stated repeatedly, including: He said three times: «لیُظهِره على الدّین كلّه» (94) (Islam will prevail over all religions). In another place, He said: «انّ الارض یرثها عبادىَ الصالحون» (95) (My righteous servants shall inherit the earth). And in another place, we read: «والعاقبة للتّقوى» (96) or «والعاقبة للمتّقین» (97) (And the end is for piety/the righteous). The meaning of the succession (vicegerency) of the believers is either succession from Allah on earth, or replacement of previous nations. Qurtubi, in his Tafsir, narrates several Hadiths regarding the complete victory of Islam and says: There will be no house on earth except that Islam will enter that house. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, during his final Hajj on the return journey from Mecca (the eighteenth of Dhu al-Hijjah) at Ghadir Khumm, appointed Hadrat Ali عليه السلام as his successor by the command of Allah. The Ayah was revealed: "Today I have approved Islam for you as a complete religion"; «رَضیتُ لكم الاسلام دیناً» (98). In this Ayah, too, Allah’s promise is the victory of the religion He has approved; « دینهم الّذِى ارتضى لهم». Therefore, the Islam that will encompass the world is the Islam of Ghadir Khumm.

93) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 94) Tawbah, 33; Fath, 28 and Saff, 9. 95) Anbiya, 105. 96) Taha, 132. 97) A'raf, 128. 98) Ma'idah, 3.

Messages

1- Islam is the future religion of the world. The future of history is in favor of the believers and the defeat of the dominion of the disbelievers. «وعداللّه... لیمكننّ لهم دینهم»

2- Give hope to the deprived believers so that pressures and hardships do not cause them despair. «وعد اللّه الّذین آمنوا»

3- The key and secret to worthiness for taking hold of the vast righteous government is only faith and righteous deeds. «الّذین آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات» The promise of victory is given to those who possess faith and deeds, not to those who only possess faith.

4- Religion is not separate from politics; rather, politics and governance are for the preservation of religion. «لیستخلفنّهم ... یعبدوننى»

5- In propagating and guiding people, mentioning examples from past history is useful for strengthening their belief. «كما استخلف الّذین من قبلهم»

6- The ultimate victory of the people of truth is a Divine tradition (Sunnah). «كما استخلف الّذین من قبلهم»

7- The goal of the victory and governance of the people of faith is the establishment of the Divine religion on earth and the attainment of complete Monotheism (Tawhid) and security. «لیمكّننّ لهم دینهم - لیبدلّنهم - لایشركون»

8- We must see the Hand of Allah in historical transformations. «وعداللّه ... لیمكّننّ لهم - لیبدلّنهم»

9- The religion of Islam is the only religion that has Divine approval. «دینهم الّذى ارتضى لهم»

10- True security is under the shadow of religious governance. «ولیبدلّنّهم من بعد خوفهم أمنا»

11- Security in the Islamic society provides suitable conditions for worship. «أمنا یعبدوننى» (The ultimate goal of the Divine promises is the sincere worship of Allah.)

12- The worship of Allah is complete only when there is no form of polytheism (Shirk) alongside it. «یعبدوننى لایشركون»

13- Even under the governance of the righteous, a group will deviate. «وعداللّه ... و من كفر بعد ذلك»

Ayahs

وَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتُوا۟ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱلرَّسُولَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ (٥٦)
Maintain the prayer and give the zakat, and obey the Apostle so that you may receive [Allah’s] mercy. (56)
لَا تَحْسَبَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مُعْجِزِينَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَمَأْوَىٰهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ ۖ وَلَبِئْسَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (٥۷)
Do not suppose that those who are faithless can frustrate [Allah] on the earth. Their refuge shall be the Fire, and it is surely an evil destination. (57)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, reciting Ayah 56, said: Zakat al-Fitrah (the Zakat of breaking the fast) is an instance of the Zakat mentioned in the Ayah and is obligatory. (99) Perhaps Ayah 57 is an answer to the question: Given all the disbelief (kufr), hypocrisy (nifaq), and resources that the opponents possess, how can Allah promise the believers sovereignty over the earth? This Ayah states: «ولا تحسبنّ الّذین كفروا معجزین فى الارض» (And never think that the disbelievers are going to escape in the earth). Do not doubt, because the opponents cannot disrupt or frustrate the will and path of Allah anywhere on earth. Contrary to the desire and false assumption of the enemies, the government of Imam Mahdi عليه السلام will one day be realized. (100)

99) Bihar, vol. 93, p. 104. 100) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Prayer (Salat), Zakat, and obedience to the Divine Leader are clear examples of the faith and righteous deeds promised sovereignty in the previous Ayah. «وعد اللّه الّذین آمنوا... اقیموا الصلوة و...»

2- In Islam, devotional matters — «اقیموا الصلوة» (Establish the Prayer), financial matters — «آتوا الزكوة» (Give the Zakat), and political and governmental matters — «اطعیوا الرسول» (Obey the Messenger) are not separate from one another.

3- Divine promises (mentioned in the previous Ayah) should not lead to idleness! «اقیموا... آتوا الزكوة...»

4- The condition for attaining Divine Mercy is following Allah and the Messenger. «لعلّكم ترحمون»

5- Disbelievers (Kuffar), even if they are superpowers, will be defeated in their struggle against the religion of Allah. «لاتحسبنّ الّذین كفروا معجزین...»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لِيَسْتَـْٔذِنكُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُكُمْ وَٱلَّذِينَ لَمْ يَبْلُغُوا۟ ٱلْحُلُمَ مِنكُمْ ثَلَـٰثَ مَرَّٰتٍ ۚ مِّن قَبْلِ صَلَوٰةِ ٱلْفَجْرِ وَحِينَ تَضَعُونَ ثِيَابَكُم مِّنَ ٱلظَّهِيرَةِ وَمِنۢ بَعْدِ صَلَوٰةِ ٱلْعِشَآءِ ۚ ثَلَـٰثُ عَوْرَٰتٍ لَّكُمْ ۚ لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَلَا عَلَيْهِمْ جُنَاحٌۢ بَعْدَهُنَّ ۚ طَوَّٰفُونَ عَلَيْكُم بَعْضُكُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ (٥۸)
O you who have faith! Your slaves and any of you who have not yet reached puberty should seek your permission three times: before the dawn prayer, and when you put off your garments at noon, and after the night prayer. These are three times of privacy for you. Apart from these, it is not sinful of you or them to frequent one another [freely]. Thus does Allah clarify the signs for you, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (58)

Key Points

If we look at the verses from the beginning of the Surah once again, we will see that the policy of preventing indecency (fahsha) is inherent in the verses. For example: Punish the adulterous man and woman in front of others (Ayah 1). Restrict their marriage (Ayah 3). If a false accusation is made without four witnesses, administer eighty lashes (Ayah 4). Those who slander the Prophet's wife will face a great punishment (Ayah 11). If you hear a slander, confront it (Ayah 12). Whoever loves the spread of indecency will face a painful punishment (Ayah 19). Do not follow the footsteps of Satan (Ayah 21). Those who accuse chaste women will face a great punishment (Ayah 23). Vile speech comes from vile people, not from you (Ayah 26). Do not enter others' homes unannounced, and if they tell you to return, accept and return (Ayah 27-28). Men and women should lower their gaze from non-mahram (unrelated individuals) and not stare (Ayah 30-31). Women should not display their adornments or stamp their feet, and strive to arrange marriages for those who are single, and do not fear poverty (Ayah 32). And in this Ayah, He says: During the hours when a person is alone with his spouse, even children and slaves should not enter unannounced. Yes, all these commands are for preventing indecency (fahsha), premature puberty, and preserving boundaries, modesty (haya), and honor (abroo).

Messages

1- Believing parents are responsible for teaching religious rulings to their children. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا لیستأذنكم»

2- The sleeping area of the parents must be separate and out of sight of the children; otherwise, permission would not be necessary. «لیستأذنكم»

3- Childhood and servitude are not excuses for violating the boundaries of others. «لیستأذنكم»

4- A man must dedicate certain hours of the day and night to his wife, and children should not interfere. «لیستأذنكم»

5- Children also have duties (taklif). «لیستأذنكم ... الّذین لم یبلغوا الحُلُم»

6- Islam is a religion of life and possesses complete comprehensiveness. It has plans and programs both for minor issues, such as a child entering the parents' room (mentioned in this very Ayah), and for major issues, such as establishing a global government (mentioned in Ayah 55).

7- Children must learn chastity from the home environment. «لیستأذنكم»

8- We should begin the training and teaching of Islamic manners before children reach puberty. «الّذین لم یبلغوا الحُلُم»

9- At home, work clothes and outdoor clothes should be set aside. «تضعون ثیابكم»

10- Rest in the afternoon (midday) and during working hours is beneficial and appropriate. «تضعون ثیابكم من الظهیرة»

11- Time management in the lives of Muslims must be based on the times of prayer. «قبل صلوة الفجر - بعد صلوة العشاء»

12- All divine rulings are based on wisdom. «علیم حكیم»

Ayah

وَإِذَا بَلَغَ ٱلْأَطْفَـٰلُ مِنكُمُ ٱلْحُلُمَ فَلْيَسْتَـْٔذِنُوا۟ كَمَا ٱسْتَـْٔذَنَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ (٥۹)
When your children reach puberty, let them ask permission [at all times] just as those [who grew up] before them asked permission. Thus does Allah clarify His signs for you, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (59)

Key Points

The words hulum (حُلُم) and ihtilam (احتلام) mean becoming junub (ritually impure) in sleep, which is one of the signs of puberty (adulthood). Of course, the signs of puberty differ for girls and boys, and these are detailed in the treatises (risalahs) of the Maraji' al-Taqlid (sources of emulation). The previous Ayah specified three particular times for children to seek permission, but this Ayah has not declared a specific time, and adult children must seek permission to enter their parents' room at all times.

To eradicate a social corruption, such as acts contrary to chastity, merely resorting to the implementation of hudud (prescribed punishments) and lashing is insufficient. Such an approach will not yield the desired result in any social issue. Rather, a comprehensive program must be arranged, consisting of intellectual and cultural education mixed with moral and emotional etiquette, sound Islamic teachings, and the creation of a healthy social environment. Only then should punishment be considered as one factor alongside these others.

For this reason, this Surah An-Noor, which is essentially the Surah of Chastity, begins with the punishment of adulterous men and women, and then expands to other issues such as providing means for healthy marriage, observing Islamic hijab (veiling), forbidding leering, prohibiting the accusation of individuals regarding sexual impurity, and finally, the seeking of permission by children when entering their parents' rooms. This shows that Islam has not neglected any of the subtleties related to this issue.

Servants and adult children are also obliged not to enter without permission. Even non-adult children who are constantly with their parents should be taught not to enter without permission during their parents' rest times. This is an Islamic etiquette, although unfortunately, it is rarely observed today. Despite the fact that the Qur’an explicitly states it in the above verses, it is also rarely seen discussed in writings and speeches concerning this Islamic ruling and its philosophy. It is unclear why this definitive ruling of the Qur’an has been subjected to neglect and inattention.

It is necessary for fathers and mothers to take these matters seriously and accustom their children to seek permission upon entering. They should also avoid actions that cause the arousal of children, including, as far as possible, a man and woman sleeping in a room where discerning children are sleeping. They must know that these matters are extremely influential in their children's destiny from an educational perspective.

In a hadith from the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, we read: «ایّاكم و ان یجامع الرجل امراته و الصبى فى المهد ینظر الیهما»(101) Beware of engaging in sexual intercourse while a child in the cradle is looking at you.(102)

101) Bihar, Vol. 103, p. 295. 102) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- Ihtilam (wet dream) is a sign of reaching religious puberty (bulugh shar'i). «و اذا بلغ الاطفال... الحُلُم»

2- Elders and those around them are role models for adolescents. «كما استأذن الّذین من قبلهم»

3- Educate your children through your actions. «كما استأذن الّذین من قبلهم»

4- The commands that Allah has issued for preserving chastity are knowledgeable and wise. «علیم حكیم»

Ayah

وَٱلْقَوَٰعِدُ مِنَ ٱلنِّسَآءِ ٱلَّـٰتِى لَا يَرْجُونَ نِكَاحًا فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِنَّ جُنَاحٌ أَن يَضَعْنَ ثِيَابَهُنَّ غَيْرَ مُتَبَرِّجَـٰتٍۭ بِزِينَةٍ ۖ وَأَن يَسْتَعْفِفْنَ خَيْرٌ لَّهُنَّ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (٦٠)
As for women advanced in years who do not expect to marry, there will be no sin upon them if they put off their cloaks, without displaying their adornment. But it is better for them to be modest, and Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing. (60)

Key Points

It is understood from the verses of the Quran that the hijab (veiling) and covering of women in the presence of non-mahram men should not be provocative or sexually stimulating, because only the following cases are exempted: * «غیر اولى الاربة»(103) one who has no sexual desire. * «او الطفل الّذین لم یظهروا على عَورات النساء» a child who knows nothing of sexual desire. * «لا یَرجون نكاحاً» elderly women who have no hope of marriage.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding «یضعن ثیابهنّ»: The intention is the removal of the head covering and outer garment, not nudity.(104)

The principle of hijab is among the fixed and essential rulings of Islam, but the type of covering should not stimulate sexual desires; rather, it should be a factor in preserving chastity and purity.

103) نور، 31. 104) تفسیر نورالثقلین ؛ كافى، ج 57، ص 522.

Messages

1- The laws of Islam correspond to realities, necessities, needs, and capabilities. (The matter must be made easy for an elderly woman who has no desire for a spouse and does not adorn herself.) «و القواعد من النساء الَّتى لا یرجون نكاحاً... ان یضعن ثیابهن»

2- For women, whether old or young, displaying adornment and bodily decoration is forbidden. «غیر متبرّجات بزینة»

3- Old age alone is not sufficient; there must also be a lack of desire for marriage in order for them to be permitted to remove their head covering. «القواعد... لایرجون نكاحاً»

4- Hijab is a means of preserving chastity. «أن یستعففن خیر لهن»

5 - Hijab is beneficial for women. «خیرٌ لهن»

6- Alongside freedom, a warning is necessary. (If divine rulings are abused, Allah is Hearing and Knowing, and He will hold the transgressors accountable). «واللّه سمیع علیم»

Ayah

لَّيْسَ عَلَى ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ حَرَجٌ وَلَا عَلَى ٱلْأَعْرَجِ حَرَجٌ وَلَا عَلَى ٱلْمَرِيضِ حَرَجٌ وَلَا عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَن تَأْكُلُوا۟ مِنۢ بُيُوتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ ءَابَآئِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أُمَّهَـٰتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ إِخْوَٰنِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أَخَوَٰتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أَعْمَـٰمِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ عَمَّـٰتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أَخْوَٰلِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ خَـٰلَـٰتِكُمْ أَوْ مَا مَلَكْتُم مَّفَاتِحَهُۥٓ أَوْ صَدِيقِكُمْ ۚ لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ أَن تَأْكُلُوا۟ جَمِيعًا أَوْ أَشْتَاتًا ۚ فَإِذَا دَخَلْتُم بُيُوتًا فَسَلِّمُوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِكُمْ تَحِيَّةً مِّنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ مُبَـٰرَكَةً طَيِّبَةً ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ (٦۱)
There is no blame upon the blind, nor any blame upon the lame, nor any blame upon the sick, nor upon yourselves if you eat from your own houses, or your fathers’ houses, or your mothers’ houses, or your brothers’ houses, or your sisters’ houses, or the houses of your paternal uncles, or the houses of your paternal aunts, or the houses of your maternal uncles, or the houses of your maternal aunts, or those whose keys are in your possession, or those of your friends. There will be no blame on you whether you eat together or separately. So when you enter houses, greet yourselves with a salutation from Allah, blessed and good. Thus does Allah clarify His signs for you so that you may exercise your reason. (61)

Key Points

Imam Baqir عليه السلام says: Before Islam, the blind, the lame, and the sick did not have the right to eat with healthy people. This Ayah permitted them to eat with healthy people collectively, or separately if they wished. (105) Allamah Tabataba'i says: The apparent meaning of the Ayah is that Allah has established a right for the believers to eat and consume from the houses of their children, relatives, and friends to the extent of their need. This permission is general and comprehensive, but the beginning of the Ayah refers to three groups who usually cannot provide their own food and are dependent on others. However, the ruling is not exclusive to these three groups but applies to all believers. (106) This Ayah only mentions permission to use the houses of blood relatives (nasabi) and does not name in-laws (sababi) such as the father-in-law, mother-in-law, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law. This Ayah is only about eating food, but other uses are conditional upon obtaining permission. Furthermore, eating food from the mentioned houses must be natural and far from extravagance and waste. (107) Even in the cases mentioned in the Ayah, sometimes circumstantial evidence indicates that these eleven groups do not wish for us to eat their food, such as when they have hidden the food in a specific place. In such cases, we must refrain. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: When a person enters a house and greets its occupants (gives salam), and they respond, this greeting is upon oneself. (108) The house of the child is not mentioned in this Ayah, perhaps because this case is clear and established. We read in a hadith: «انت و مالك لابیك» (You and your wealth belong to your father).

105) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. 106) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 107) Tafsir Safi. 108) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- We should prioritize others over ourselves, especially the deprived. «الاعمى - الاعرج - المریض - انفسكم»

2- Family and kinship ties influence legal matters. (Although the food is the property of the homeowner, Islam has made kinship and emotional ties prevail over individual ownership in these cases.) «أن تأكلوا من... بیوت اعمامكم»

3- Customary evidence and rational certainty are sufficient for the permissibility of consuming from the houses of others. (For example, giving the house key indicates consent.) «ملكتم مفاتحه او صدیقكم»

4- In Islam, the right of a friend is placed alongside the right of a relative. «اعمامكم... او صدیقكم» (Plato was asked: Do you love your brother more or your friend? He replied: My brother, when he is my friend.) (109)

5- Giving salam (greeting) is among the manners of entering a house. «اذا دخلتم بیوتاً فسلّموا»

6- Salam is a heavenly, blessed, and pleasing mannerism. «تحیّة من عند اللّه مباركة طیّبة»

7- Giving salam is effective in the goodness and blessing of life. «مباركة طیّبة»

8- The occupants of the house are equivalent to your own selves. «سلّموا على انفسكم»

9- Allah must determine the boundaries of rights and entitlements. «یبین اللّه لكم الآیات»

10- One must reflect upon divine rulings. «لعلّكم تعقلون»

109) Tafsir Maraghi.

Ayah

إِنَّمَا ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ وَإِذَا كَانُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ عَلَىٰٓ أَمْرٍ جَامِعٍ لَّمْ يَذْهَبُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ يَسْتَـْٔذِنُوهُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَسْتَـْٔذِنُونَكَ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ ۚ فَإِذَا ٱسْتَـْٔذَنُوكَ لِبَعْضِ شَأْنِهِمْ فَأْذَن لِّمَن شِئْتَ مِنْهُمْ وَٱسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمُ ٱللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (٦۲)
Indeed the faithful are those who have faith in Allah and His Apostle, and when they are with him in a collective affair, they do not leave until they have sought his permission. Indeed those who seek your permission are those who have faith in Allah and His Apostle. So when they seek your permission for some of theirs [private] work, give permission to whomever of them you wish and plead with Allah to forgive them. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (62)

Key Points

Various points regarding the occasion of revelation (shān nuzūl) of this Ayah have been mentioned in the books of exegesis (Tafsir). We present the main cases here: 1- This Ayah relates to "Hanzalah," whose wedding night coincided with the command to move toward Uhud. He sought permission from the presence of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, went home, performed the marriage rites (zawāf), and went to the front line without performing ghusl (ritual bath), where he attained martyrdom. The Prophet said: "The angels washed him." Allah granted him a child from that single night Hanzalah spent with his wife, and the story of that child's life is detailed. (110) 2- This Ayah is about the hypocrites (munāfiqīn), who would stand for prayer whenever they were with people, but if no one saw them and they were alone, they would flee from prayer. (111) 3- The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله dispatched a group for a task, but they went after their personal affairs, and this Ayah was revealed concerning them. 4- This Ayah concerns Muslims who were busy digging the trench (Khandaq) and sought permission from the Prophet to check on their families. Seeking permission from the leader is a sign of etiquette, submission, adherence to law, love for the leader, and order and discipline.

110) Tafsir al-Mizan. 111) Tafsir Kabir Fakhr Razi.

Messages

1- Presence alongside the leadership to solve problems is a sign of faith (Iman), and abandoning it is a sign of hypocrisy (nifāq) or weak faith. «انّما المؤمنون الّذین... كانوا معه على امر جامع»

2- In organizational matters where decisions are made based on consensus and consultation, self-centeredness and autonomy are forbidden. «على امر جامع لم یذهبوا»

3- In social life, the existence of a leader and following him is necessary. «على امر جامع لم یذهبوا»

4- Faith must be accompanied by submission and obedience to the leadership. «لم یذهبوا حتّى یستأذنوه»

5- We must value the social system and group work. «لم یذهبوا حتّى یستأذنوه»

6- Some matters must be stated vaguely (or kept private). «لبعض شأنهم»

7- Leadership in Islam is accompanied by prudence and mercy. «فأذن لمن شئت منهم - و استغفر لهم»

8- The final decision rests with the leader. «لمَن شئت منهم» (The Prophet holds authority (wilāyah) over the people in societal matters).

9- The leader must be flexible and realistic in implementing His policies. «فأذن لمن شئت منهم و استغفر لهم اللّه»

10- The Prophet's permission makes the action permissible (halāl), but the very act of abandoning public duties requires seeking forgiveness (Istighfār). «فأذن... واستغفر لهم»

11- Raising personal issues while engaged in public duties, even if with permission, is not ideal and requires apology (or seeking forgiveness). «واستغفر لهم»

12- The Istighfār and supplication (Du'ā) of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله for others are answered. «واستغفر لهم اللّه اِنّ اللّه غفور رحیم»

Ayah

لَّا تَجْعَلُوا۟ دُعَآءَ ٱلرَّسُولِ بَيْنَكُمْ كَدُعَآءِ بَعْضِكُم بَعْضًا ۚ قَدْ يَعْلَمُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَتَسَلَّلُونَ مِنكُمْ لِوَاذًا ۚ فَلْيَحْذَرِ ٱلَّذِينَ يُخَالِفُونَ عَنْ أَمْرِهِۦٓ أَن تُصِيبَهُمْ فِتْنَةٌ أَوْ يُصِيبَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (٦۳)
Do not consider the Apostle’s summons amongst you to be like your summoning one another. Allah certainly knows those of you who slip away shielding one another. Those who disobey his orders should beware lest an affliction should visit them or a painful punishment should befall them. (63)

Key Points

'Tasallul' is secret departure, and 'Liwādh' is hiding behind another person, which was the action of the hypocrites. Regarding the meaning of this Ayah, there are four possibilities: 1- Do not call the Prophet by name, like you call others. In a narration, we read: Do not say "Yā Muhammad" or "Yā Abā al-Qāsim," but rather say "Yā Rasūl Allāh" (O Messenger of Allah) and "Yā Nabī Allāh" (O Prophet of Allah). (112) In another narration, it is stated that this Ayah also applies to the infallible successors (Imams) of that حضرت. (113) 2- Do not call the Prophet with a loud voice, like you call others. (114) 3- Do not consider the Prophet's invitation like the invitation of others. If the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله invites you to an action, such as going to the battlefield or performing prayer, you must obey and do not take his invitation lightly. 4- Do not consider the Prophet's supplication like that of others. The curse and the supplication of the Prophet are answered; be careful. (115)

(112) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn. (113) Bihār, Vol. 23, p. 301. (114) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayān. (115) Tafsir Kabīr of Fakhr al-Rāzī.

Messages

1- Divine leadership has sanctity. Both mention his name with respect and be completely submissive to him. «لا تجعلوا دعاء الرسول...»

2- Taking the leader's messages lightly and ignoring them leads to trial (fitnah) and painful punishment ('adhāb). «فتنة او... عذاب الیم»

3- Opposing the command of Allah and the Messenger leads either to worldly trial (fitnah) or otherworldly punishment. «یخالفون عن امره... فتنة او تصیبهم عذاب الیم»

Ayah

أَلَآ إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ قَدْ يَعْلَمُ مَآ أَنتُمْ عَلَيْهِ وَيَوْمَ يُرْجَعُونَ إِلَيْهِ فَيُنَبِّئُهُم بِمَا عَمِلُوا۟ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌۢ (٦٤)
Behold! To Allah indeed belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. He certainly knows your present state. The day they are brought back to Him He will inform them about what they have done, and Allah has knowledge of all things. (64)

Messages

1- Allah is aware of all thoughts and intentions. «قد یعلم... فینبّئهم»

2- Belief in Allah's knowledge is a means of deterring evil. «یعلم ما انتم علیه... فینبّئهم» «والحمدللّه ربّ العالمین»
